````python
275: 
276: @dataclass
277: class ExpertiseConfig:
278:     """Complete configurable expertise definition.
279: 
280:     Expertise configurations define domain-specific knowledge for entity
281:     extraction, including entity types, relationship types, correlation rules,
282:     and inference rules. All expertise is loaded from configuration (YAML/JSON)
283:     or defined programmatically - no hard-coded domain knowledge.
284: 
285:     Example usage:
286:         # Load from file
287:         loader = ExpertiseLoader()
288:         expertise = loader.load_file("saas_expert.yaml")
289: 
290:         # Use with MemoryLake
291:         async with MemoryLake() as lake:
292:             result = await lake.remember(content, expertise=expertise)
293: 
294:         # Or define programmatically
295:         expertise = ExpertiseConfig(
296:             name="custom",
297:             system_prompt="You are an expert in...",
298:             entity_types=[EntityTypeConfig(name="CUSTOM", description="...")],
299:         )
300:     """
301: 
302:     name: str
303:     version: str = "1.0.0"
304:     description: str = ""
305:     extends: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Inherit from other configs
306: 
307:     # LLM prompts (Jinja2 templates supported)
308:     system_prompt: str | None = None
309:     extraction_prompt: str | None = None
310: 
311:     # Type definitions
312:     entity_types: list[EntityTypeConfig] = field(default_factory=list)
313:     relationship_types: list[RelationshipTypeConfig] = field(default_factory=list)
314: 
315:     # Tool-specific knowledge (arbitrary dict for schema info)
316:     tool_schemas: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)
317: 
318:     # Cross-tool correlation rules
319:     correlation_rules: list[CorrelationRule] = field(default_factory=list)
320: 
321:     # Inference rules for semantic expansion
322:     inference_rules: list[InferenceRule] = field(default_factory=list)
323: 
324:     # Confidence thresholds
325:     confidence: ConfidenceConfig = field(default_factory=ConfidenceConfig)
326: 
327:     # Expansion settings
328:     expansion: ExpansionConfig = field(default_factory=ExpansionConfig)
329: 
330:     # Additional metadata
331:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
332: 
333:     def get_entity_type_names(self) -> list[str]:
334:         """Get list of entity type names."""
335:         return [et.name for et in self.entity_types]
336: 
337:     def get_relationship_type_names(self) -> list[str]:
338:         """Get list of relationship type names."""
339:         return [rt.name for rt in self.relationship_types]
340: 
341:     def get_entity_type(self, name: str) -> EntityTypeConfig | None:
342:         """Get entity type config by name."""
343:         for et in self.entity_types:
344:             if et.name == name:
345:                 return et
346:         return None
347: 
348:     def get_relationship_type(self, name: str) -> RelationshipTypeConfig | None:
349:         """Get relationship type config by name."""
350:         for rt in self.relationship_types:
351:             if rt.name == name:
352:                 return rt
353:         return None
354: 
355:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
356:         """Convert to dictionary representation."""
357:         return {
358:             "name": self.name,
359:             "version": self.version,
360:             "description": self.description,
361:             "extends": self.extends,
362:             "system_prompt": self.system_prompt,
363:             "extraction_prompt": self.extraction_prompt,
364:             "entity_types": [et.to_dict() for et in self.entity_types],
365:             "relationship_types": [rt.to_dict() for rt in self.relationship_types],
366:             "tool_schemas": self.tool_schemas,
367:             "correlation_rules": [cr.to_dict() for cr in self.correlation_rules],
368:             "inference_rules": [ir.to_dict() for ir in self.inference_rules],
369:             "confidence": self.confidence.to_dict(),
370:             "expansion": self.expansion.to_dict(),
371:             "metadata": self.metadata,
372:         }
373: 
374:     @classmethod
375:     def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> ExpertiseConfig:
376:         """Create expertise config from dictionary."""
377:         entity_types = [
378:             EntityTypeConfig.from_dict(et) if isinstance(et, dict) else et for et in data.get("entity_types", [])
379:         ]
380:         relationship_types = [
381:             RelationshipTypeConfig.from_dict(rt) if isinstance(rt, dict) else rt
382:             for rt in data.get("relationship_types", [])
383:         ]
384:         correlation_rules = [
385:             CorrelationRule.from_dict(cr) if isinstance(cr, dict) else cr for cr in data.get("correlation_rules", [])
386:         ]
387:         inference_rules = [
388:             InferenceRule.from_dict(ir) if isinstance(ir, dict) else ir for ir in data.get("inference_rules", [])
389:         ]
390: 
391:         confidence_data = data.get("confidence", {})
392:         confidence = (
393:             ConfidenceConfig.from_dict(confidence_data) if isinstance(confidence_data, dict) else confidence_data
394:         )
395: 
396:         expansion_data = data.get("expansion", {})
397:         expansion = ExpansionConfig.from_dict(expansion_data) if isinstance(expansion_data, dict) else expansion_data
398: 
399:         return cls(
400:             name=data.get("name", "custom"),
401:             version=data.get("version", "1.0.0"),
402:             description=data.get("description", ""),
403:             extends=data.get("extends", []),
404:             system_prompt=data.get("system_prompt"),
405:             extraction_prompt=data.get("extraction_prompt"),
406:             entity_types=entity_types,
407:             relationship_types=relationship_types,
408:             tool_schemas=data.get("tool_schemas", {}),
409:             correlation_rules=correlation_rules,
410:             inference_rules=inference_rules,
411:             confidence=confidence if isinstance(confidence, ConfidenceConfig) else ConfidenceConfig(),
412:             expansion=expansion if isinstance(expansion, ExpansionConfig) else ExpansionConfig(),
413:             metadata=data.get("metadata", {}),
414:         )
415: 
416:     def to_extraction_skill(self) -> ExtractionSkill:
417:         """Convert to a legacy ExtractionSkill for backward compatibility."""
418:         return ExtractionSkill(
419:             name=self.name,
420:             description=self.description,
421:             entity_types=self.get_entity_type_names(),
422:             relationship_types=self.get_relationship_type_names(),
423:             custom_prompt=self.extraction_prompt,
424:             min_entity_confidence=self.confidence.min_entity,
425:             min_relationship_confidence=self.confidence.min_relationship,
426:             metadata=self.metadata,
427:         )
428: 
429: 
430: @dataclass
431: class ExtractionSkill:
432:     """Configurable extraction skill definition.
433: 
434:     Skills define what types of entities and relationships to extract
435:     from documents. They can be customized per namespace or document type.
436:     """
437: 
438:     name: str
439:     description: str = ""
440: 
441:     # Entity extraction configuration
442:     entity_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
443:     relationship_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
444: 
445:     # Custom extraction prompt (optional)
446:     custom_prompt: str | None = None
447: 
448:     # Processing configuration
449:     extract_entities: bool = True
450:     extract_relationships: bool = True
451: 
452:     # Confidence thresholds
453:     min_entity_confidence: float = 0.5
454:     min_relationship_confidence: float = 0.5
455: 
456:     # Additional metadata
457:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
458: 
459:     @classmethod
460:     def general_entities(cls) -> ExtractionSkill:
461:         """Create a general entity extraction skill."""
462:         return cls(
463:             name="general_entities",
464:             description="Extract general entities like people, organizations, and concepts",
465:             entity_types=["PERSON", "ORGANIZATION", "CONCEPT", "LOCATION"],
466:             relationship_types=["WORKS_FOR", "KNOWS", "RELATES_TO", "LOCATED_IN"],
467:         )
468: 
469:     @classmethod
470:     def technical_docs(cls) -> ExtractionSkill:
471:         """Create a skill for technical documentation."""
472:         return cls(
473:             name="technical_docs",
474:             description="Extract technical entities from documentation",
475:             entity_types=["TECHNOLOGY", "CONCEPT", "PRODUCT", "ORGANIZATION"],
476:             relationship_types=["DEPENDS_ON", "IMPLEMENTS", "PART_OF", "RELATES_TO"],
477:         )
478: 
479:     @classmethod
480:     def business_intel(cls) -> ExtractionSkill:
481:         """Create a skill for business intelligence."""
482:         return cls(
483:             name="business_intel",
484:             description="Extract business entities and relationships",
485:             entity_types=["PERSON", "ORGANIZATION", "PRODUCT", "EVENT", "LOCATION"],
486:             relationship_types=[
487:                 "WORKS_FOR",
488:                 "MANAGES",
489:                 "OWNS",
490:                 "COMPETES_WITH",
491:                 "PARTNERS_WITH",
492:                 "HEADQUARTERED_IN",
493:             ],
494:         )
495: 
496:     @classmethod
497:     def research_papers(cls) -> ExtractionSkill:
498:         """Create a skill for research papers."""
499:         return cls(
500:             name="research_papers",
501:             description="Extract entities from academic research",
502:             entity_types=["PERSON", "ORGANIZATION", "CONCEPT", "TECHNOLOGY", "EVENT"],
503:             relationship_types=[
504:                 "COLLABORATES_WITH",
505:                 "DERIVED_FROM",
506:                 "IMPLEMENTS",
507:                 "RELATES_TO",
508:                 "PRECEDES",
509:             ],
510:         )
511: 
512:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
513:         """Convert skill to dictionary."""
514:         return {
515:             "name": self.name,
516:             "description": self.description,
517:             "entity_types": self.entity_types,
518:             "relationship_types": self.relationship_types,
519:             "custom_prompt": self.custom_prompt,
520:             "extract_entities": self.extract_entities,
521:             "extract_relationships": self.extract_relationships,
522:             "min_entity_confidence": self.min_entity_confidence,
523:             "min_relationship_confidence": self.min_relationship_confidence,
524:             "metadata": self.metadata,
525:         }
526: 
527:     @classmethod
528:     def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> ExtractionSkill:
529:         """Create skill from dictionary."""
530:         return cls(
531:             name=data.get("name", "custom"),
532:             description=data.get("description", ""),
533:             entity_types=data.get("entity_types", []),
534:             relationship_types=data.get("relationship_types", []),
535:             custom_prompt=data.get("custom_prompt"),
536:             extract_entities=data.get("extract_entities", True),
537:             extract_relationships=data.get("extract_relationships", True),
538:             min_entity_confidence=data.get("min_entity_confidence", 0.5),
539:             min_relationship_confidence=data.get("min_relationship_confidence", 0.5),
540:             metadata=data.get("metadata", {}),
541:         )
````

## File: src/khora/extraction/skills/composer.py
````python
  1: """Expertise configuration composer.
  2: 
  3: Handles merging and inheritance of expertise configurations.
  4: """
  5: 
  6: from __future__ import annotations
  7: 
  8: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
  9: 
 10: from loguru import logger
 11: 
 12: from .base import (
 13:     ConfidenceConfig,
 14:     CorrelationRule,
 15:     EntityTypeConfig,
 16:     ExpansionConfig,
 17:     ExpertiseConfig,
 18:     InferenceRule,
 19:     RelationshipTypeConfig,
 20: )
 21: 
 22: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 23:     from .loader import ExpertiseLoader
 24: 
 25: 
 26: class ExpertiseComposer:
 27:     """Compose and merge expertise configurations.
 28: 
 29:     Handles:
 30:     - Merging multiple configurations
 31:     - Resolving 'extends' inheritance chains
 32:     - Combining entity types, relationship types, rules
 33:     - Jinja2 template rendering
 34: 
 35:     Merge strategy:
 36:     - Entity types: Later configs add new types or override existing by name
 37:     - Relationship types: Same as entity types
 38:     - Correlation rules: Combined, later takes precedence on name conflict
 39:     - Inference rules: Combined, later takes precedence on name conflict
 40:     - Prompts: Later config's prompts override earlier
 41:     - Tool schemas: Deep merged, later values take precedence
 42:     - Confidence: Later config overrides
 43:     - Expansion: Later config overrides
 44:     """
 45: 
 46:     def __init__(self, loader: ExpertiseLoader | None = None) -> None:
 47:         """Initialize the composer.
 48: 
 49:         Args:
 50:             loader: ExpertiseLoader for resolving 'extends' references
 51:         """
 52:         self._loader = loader
 53: 
 54:     def merge(self, configs: list[ExpertiseConfig]) -> ExpertiseConfig:
 55:         """Merge multiple expertise configurations.
 56: 
 57:         Configurations are merged in order, with later configs taking
 58:         precedence for conflicting values.
 59: 
 60:         Args:
 61:             configs: List of configurations to merge
 62: 
 63:         Returns:
 64:             Merged ExpertiseConfig
 65:         """
 66:         if not configs:
 67:             raise ValueError("Cannot merge empty list of configurations")
 68: 
 69:         if len(configs) == 1:
 70:             return configs[0]
 71: 
 72:         # Start with first config
 73:         result = self._copy_config(configs[0])
 74: 
 75:         # Merge each subsequent config
 76:         for config in configs[1:]:
 77:             result = self._merge_two(result, config)
 78: 
 79:         return result
 80: 
 81:     def resolve_and_merge(self, config: ExpertiseConfig) -> ExpertiseConfig:
 82:         """Resolve 'extends' references and merge with parents.
 83: 
 84:         Args:
 85:             config: Configuration with potential 'extends' references
 86: 
 87:         Returns:
 88:             Resolved and merged ExpertiseConfig
 89:         """
 90:         if not config.extends or not self._loader:
 91:             return config
 92: 
 93:         # Load and resolve parent configs
 94:         parents = []
 95:         for parent_source in config.extends:
 96:             try:
 97:                 parent = self._loader.load_source(parent_source)
 98:                 # Recursively resolve parent's extends
 99:                 parent = self.resolve_and_merge(parent)
100:                 parents.append(parent)
101:             except Exception as e:
102:                 logger.warning(f"Failed to load parent expertise {parent_source}: {e}")
103: 
104:         if not parents:
105:             return config
106: 
107:         # Merge parents first
108:         base = self.merge(parents) if len(parents) > 1 else parents[0]
109: 
110:         # Merge current config on top of base (excluding extends)
111:         config_without_extends = self._copy_config(config)
112:         config_without_extends.extends = []
113: 
114:         return self._merge_two(base, config_without_extends)
115: 
116:     def render_prompt(
117:         self,
118:         template: str | None,
119:         *,
120:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig | None = None,
121:         context: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
122:         parent_prompt: str | None = None,
123:     ) -> str:
124:         """Render a Jinja2 prompt template.
125: 
126:         Supported template variables:
127:         - expertise: The ExpertiseConfig object
128:         - entity_types: List of entity type configs
129:         - relationship_types: List of relationship type configs
130:         - tool_schemas: Tool schema dict
131:         - tools: List of tool names
132:         - parent_prompt: Parent's system prompt (for inheritance)
133:         - context: Any additional context passed in
134: 
135:         Args:
136:             template: Jinja2 template string
137:             expertise: ExpertiseConfig for template context
138:             context: Additional context variables
139:             parent_prompt: Parent's prompt for {{ parent_prompt }} variable
140: 
141:         Returns:
142:             Rendered prompt string
143:         """
144:         if not template:
145:             return ""
146: 
147:         try:
148:             from jinja2 import Template
149: 
150:             user_ctx = context or {}
151:             ctx = {
152:                 "expertise": expertise,
153:                 "entity_types": expertise.entity_types if expertise else [],
154:                 "relationship_types": expertise.relationship_types if expertise else [],
155:                 "tool_schemas": expertise.tool_schemas if expertise else {},
156:                 "tools": list(expertise.tool_schemas.keys()) if expertise else [],
157:                 "parent_prompt": parent_prompt or "",
158:                 "source_tool": user_ctx.get("source_tool", ""),
159:                 "tool_context": user_ctx.get("tool_context", ""),
160:                 **user_ctx,
161:             }
162: 
163:             jinja_template = Template(template)
164:             return jinja_template.render(**ctx)
165: 
166:         except ImportError:
167:             # Jinja2 not installed, return template as-is
168:             logger.debug("Jinja2 not installed, returning template without rendering")
169:             return template
170: 
171:         except Exception as e:
172:             logger.warning(f"Failed to render prompt template: {e}")
173:             return template
174: 
175:     def _merge_two(self, base: ExpertiseConfig, overlay: ExpertiseConfig) -> ExpertiseConfig:
176:         """Merge two configurations, with overlay taking precedence."""
177:         return ExpertiseConfig(
178:             name=overlay.name or base.name,
179:             version=overlay.version if overlay.version != "1.0.0" else base.version,
180:             description=overlay.description or base.description,
181:             extends=[],  # Resolved, no need to keep extends
182:             system_prompt=overlay.system_prompt or base.system_prompt,
183:             extraction_prompt=overlay.extraction_prompt or base.extraction_prompt,
184:             entity_types=self._merge_entity_types(base.entity_types, overlay.entity_types),
185:             relationship_types=self._merge_relationship_types(base.relationship_types, overlay.relationship_types),
186:             tool_schemas=self._deep_merge_dicts(base.tool_schemas, overlay.tool_schemas),
187:             correlation_rules=self._merge_correlation_rules(base.correlation_rules, overlay.correlation_rules),
188:             inference_rules=self._merge_inference_rules(base.inference_rules, overlay.inference_rules),
189:             confidence=self._merge_confidence(base.confidence, overlay.confidence),
190:             expansion=self._merge_expansion(base.expansion, overlay.expansion),
191:             metadata=self._deep_merge_dicts(base.metadata, overlay.metadata),
192:         )
193: 
194:     def _merge_entity_types(
195:         self,
196:         base: list[EntityTypeConfig],
197:         overlay: list[EntityTypeConfig],
198:     ) -> list[EntityTypeConfig]:
199:         """Merge entity type lists, overlay takes precedence on name conflict."""
200:         result: dict[str, EntityTypeConfig] = {et.name: et for et in base}
201:         for et in overlay:
202:             result[et.name] = et
203:         return list(result.values())
204: 
205:     def _merge_relationship_types(
206:         self,
207:         base: list[RelationshipTypeConfig],
208:         overlay: list[RelationshipTypeConfig],
209:     ) -> list[RelationshipTypeConfig]:
210:         """Merge relationship type lists, overlay takes precedence on name conflict."""
211:         result: dict[str, RelationshipTypeConfig] = {rt.name: rt for rt in base}
212:         for rt in overlay:
213:             result[rt.name] = rt
214:         return list(result.values())
215: 
216:     def _merge_correlation_rules(
217:         self,
218:         base: list[CorrelationRule],
219:         overlay: list[CorrelationRule],
220:     ) -> list[CorrelationRule]:
221:         """Merge correlation rule lists, overlay takes precedence on name conflict."""
222:         result: dict[str, CorrelationRule] = {cr.name: cr for cr in base}
223:         for cr in overlay:
224:             result[cr.name] = cr
225:         return list(result.values())
226: 
227:     def _merge_inference_rules(
228:         self,
229:         base: list[InferenceRule],
230:         overlay: list[InferenceRule],
231:     ) -> list[InferenceRule]:
232:         """Merge inference rule lists, overlay takes precedence on name conflict."""
233:         result: dict[str, InferenceRule] = {ir.name: ir for ir in base}
234:         for ir in overlay:
235:             result[ir.name] = ir
236:         return list(result.values())
237: 
238:     def _merge_confidence(self, base: ConfidenceConfig, overlay: ConfidenceConfig) -> ConfidenceConfig:
239:         """Merge confidence configs, overlay overrides non-default values."""
240:         return ConfidenceConfig(
241:             min_entity=overlay.min_entity if overlay.min_entity != 0.5 else base.min_entity,
242:             min_relationship=overlay.min_relationship if overlay.min_relationship != 0.5 else base.min_relationship,
243:             min_inferred=overlay.min_inferred if overlay.min_inferred != 0.3 else base.min_inferred,
244:         )
245: 
246:     def _merge_expansion(self, base: ExpansionConfig, overlay: ExpansionConfig) -> ExpansionConfig:
247:         """Merge expansion configs, overlay overrides non-default values."""
248:         return ExpansionConfig(
249:             enabled=overlay.enabled,
250:             depth=overlay.depth if overlay.depth != 2 else base.depth,
251:             cross_tool_unification=overlay.cross_tool_unification,
252:             relationship_inference=overlay.relationship_inference,
253:             max_entities_per_expansion=(
254:                 overlay.max_entities_per_expansion
255:                 if overlay.max_entities_per_expansion != 100
256:                 else base.max_entities_per_expansion
257:             ),
258:         )
259: 
260:     def _deep_merge_dicts(self, base: dict[str, Any], overlay: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
261:         """Deep merge two dictionaries, overlay takes precedence."""
262:         result = base.copy()
263:         for key, value in overlay.items():
264:             if key in result and isinstance(result[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):
265:                 result[key] = self._deep_merge_dicts(result[key], value)
266:             else:
267:                 result[key] = value
268:         return result
269: 
270:     def _copy_config(self, config: ExpertiseConfig) -> ExpertiseConfig:
271:         """Create a shallow copy of a configuration."""
272:         return ExpertiseConfig(
273:             name=config.name,
274:             version=config.version,
275:             description=config.description,
276:             extends=config.extends.copy(),
277:             system_prompt=config.system_prompt,
278:             extraction_prompt=config.extraction_prompt,
279:             entity_types=config.entity_types.copy(),
280:             relationship_types=config.relationship_types.copy(),
281:             tool_schemas=config.tool_schemas.copy(),
282:             correlation_rules=config.correlation_rules.copy(),
283:             inference_rules=config.inference_rules.copy(),
284:             confidence=config.confidence,
285:             expansion=config.expansion,
286:             metadata=config.metadata.copy(),
287:         )
````

## File: src/khora/pipelines/flows/sync.py
````python
  1: """Source synchronization flows for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Provides incremental sync from external sources with checkpoint tracking.
  4: """
  5: 
  6: from __future__ import annotations
  7: 
  8: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
  9: from uuid import UUID
 10: 
 11: from loguru import logger
 12: 
 13: from ..registry import pipeline
 14: 
 15: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 16:     from khora.storage import StorageCoordinator
 17: 
 18: 
 19: async def get_sync_checkpoint(
 20:     namespace_id: UUID,
 21:     source: str,
 22:     storage: StorageCoordinator,
 23: ) -> str | None:
 24:     """Get the last sync checkpoint for a source."""
 25:     return await storage.get_sync_checkpoint(namespace_id, source)
 26: 
 27: 
 28: async def set_sync_checkpoint(
 29:     namespace_id: UUID,
 30:     source: str,
 31:     checkpoint: str,
 32:     storage: StorageCoordinator,
 33: ) -> None:
 34:     """Set the sync checkpoint for a source."""
 35:     await storage.set_sync_checkpoint(namespace_id, source, checkpoint)
 36: 
 37: 
 38: async def fetch_from_source(
 39:     source: str,
 40:     connector_config: dict[str, Any],
 41:     checkpoint: str | None,
 42: ) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], str | None]:
 43:     """Fetch documents from an external source.
 44: 
 45:     This is a placeholder that should be replaced with actual connector
 46:     implementations for different sources (GitHub, Notion, etc.)
 47: 
 48:     Returns:
 49:         Tuple of (documents, new_checkpoint)
 50:     """
 51:     # This would be implemented by specific connectors
 52:     # For now, return empty results
 53:     logger.warning(f"No connector implemented for source: {source}")
 54:     return [], checkpoint
 55: 
 56: 
 57: @pipeline("sync_source", description="Sync from external source", tags=["sync"])
 58: async def sync_source(
 59:     namespace_id: UUID,
 60:     source: str,
 61:     storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None,
 62:     *,
 63:     connector_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 64:     skill_name: str = "general_entities",
 65:     **kwargs,
 66: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 67:     """Sync documents from an external source.
 68: 
 69:     Performs incremental sync using checkpoints to track progress.
 70: 
 71:     Args:
 72:         namespace_id: Target namespace
 73:         source: Source name (e.g., "github", "notion")
 74:         storage: StorageCoordinator instance
 75:         connector_config: Configuration for the source connector
 76:         skill_name: Extraction skill to use
 77: 
 78:     Returns:
 79:         Summary of sync results
 80:     """
 81:     from .ingest import ingest_documents
 82: 
 83:     if storage is None:
 84:         raise ValueError("storage is required")
 85: 
 86:     connector_config = connector_config or {}
 87: 
 88:     logger.info(f"Starting sync from {source} into namespace {namespace_id}")
 89: 
 90:     # Get last checkpoint
 91:     checkpoint = await get_sync_checkpoint(namespace_id, source, storage)
 92:     if checkpoint:
 93:         logger.info(f"Resuming from checkpoint: {checkpoint[:50]}...")
 94: 
 95:     # Fetch documents from source
 96:     documents, new_checkpoint = await fetch_from_source(source, connector_config, checkpoint)
 97: 
 98:     if not documents:
 99:         logger.info(f"No new documents from {source}")
100:         return {
101:             "source": source,
102:             "documents_fetched": 0,
103:             "documents_processed": 0,
104:         }
105: 
106:     logger.info(f"Fetched {len(documents)} documents from {source}")
107: 
108:     # Ingest the documents
109:     result = await ingest_documents(
110:         namespace_id=namespace_id,
111:         documents=documents,
112:         storage=storage,
113:         skill_name=skill_name,
114:         **kwargs,
115:     )
116: 
117:     # Update checkpoint
118:     if new_checkpoint:
119:         await set_sync_checkpoint(namespace_id, source, new_checkpoint, storage)
120:         logger.info(f"Updated checkpoint: {new_checkpoint[:50]}...")
121: 
122:     return {
123:         "source": source,
124:         "documents_fetched": len(documents),
125:         "documents_processed": result["processed_documents"],
126:         "checkpoint": new_checkpoint,
127:         **result,
128:     }
129: 
130: 
131: @pipeline("sync_all", description="Sync from all configured sources", tags=["sync"])
132: async def sync_all_sources(
133:     namespace_id: UUID,
134:     sources: list[dict[str, Any]],
135:     storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None,
136:     **kwargs,
137: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
138:     """Sync from multiple sources.
139: 
140:     Args:
141:         namespace_id: Target namespace
142:         sources: List of source configs with 'name' and 'config' keys
143:         storage: StorageCoordinator instance
144: 
145:     Returns:
146:         Summary of all sync results
147:     """
148:     if storage is None:
149:         raise ValueError("storage is required")
150: 
151:     results = []
152:     for source_config in sources:
153:         source_name = source_config.get("name", "unknown")
154:         connector_config = source_config.get("config", {})
155: 
156:         try:
157:             result = await sync_source(
158:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
159:                 source=source_name,
160:                 storage=storage,
161:                 connector_config=connector_config,
162:                 **kwargs,
163:             )
164:             results.append({"source": source_name, "status": "success", **result})
165:         except Exception as e:
166:             logger.error(f"Sync failed for {source_name}: {e}")
167:             results.append({"source": source_name, "status": "failed", "error": str(e)})
168: 
169:     return {
170:         "namespace_id": str(namespace_id),
171:         "sources_synced": len([r for r in results if r["status"] == "success"]),
172:         "sources_failed": len([r for r in results if r["status"] == "failed"]),
173:         "results": results,
174:     }
````

## File: src/khora/pipelines/manager.py
````python
  1: """Pipeline manager for orchestrating pipeline flows."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  6: from datetime import datetime
  7: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
  8: from uuid import UUID
  9: 
 10: from loguru import logger
 11: 
 12: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 13:     from khora.config import KhoraConfig
 14:     from khora.storage import StorageCoordinator
 15: 
 16: 
 17: @dataclass
 18: class PipelineRun:
 19:     """Information about a pipeline run."""
 20: 
 21:     run_id: str
 22:     pipeline_name: str
 23:     namespace_id: UUID
 24:     status: str  # pending, running, completed, failed
 25:     started_at: datetime | None = None
 26:     completed_at: datetime | None = None
 27:     error: str | None = None
 28:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 29: 
 30: 
 31: class PipelineManager:
 32:     """Manager for running and tracking pipelines.
 33: 
 34:     Orchestrates pipeline flows and tracks their execution
 35:     within the context of a memory namespace.
 36:     """
 37: 
 38:     def __init__(
 39:         self,
 40:         storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None,
 41:         config: KhoraConfig | None = None,
 42:     ) -> None:
 43:         """Initialize the pipeline manager.
 44: 
 45:         Args:
 46:             storage: StorageCoordinator for data access
 47:             config: Khora configuration
 48:         """
 49:         self._storage = storage
 50:         self._config = config
 51:         self._runs: dict[str, PipelineRun] = {}
 52: 
 53:     async def run_pipeline(
 54:         self,
 55:         pipeline_name: str,
 56:         namespace_id: UUID,
 57:         *,
 58:         parameters: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 59:         wait: bool = True,
 60:     ) -> PipelineRun:
 61:         """Run a registered pipeline.
 62: 
 63:         Args:
 64:             pipeline_name: Name of the pipeline to run
 65:             namespace_id: Namespace to run in
 66:             parameters: Pipeline parameters
 67:             wait: Wait for completion
 68: 
 69:         Returns:
 70:             PipelineRun with execution info
 71:         """
 72:         from .registry import get_registry
 73: 
 74:         registry = get_registry()
 75:         pipeline_info = registry.get(pipeline_name)
 76: 
 77:         if not pipeline_info:
 78:             raise ValueError(f"Unknown pipeline: {pipeline_name}")
 79: 
 80:         # Create run record
 81:         import uuid
 82: 
 83:         run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
 84:         run = PipelineRun(
 85:             run_id=run_id,
 86:             pipeline_name=pipeline_name,
 87:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 88:             status="running",
 89:             started_at=datetime.now(),
 90:         )
 91:         self._runs[run_id] = run
 92: 
 93:         logger.info(f"Starting pipeline {pipeline_name} (run_id={run_id})")
 94: 
 95:         try:
 96:             # Prepare parameters
 97:             params = parameters or {}
 98:             params["namespace_id"] = namespace_id
 99:             params["storage"] = self._storage
100:             params["config"] = self._config
101: 
102:             # Run the pipeline
103:             func = pipeline_info.func
104:             result = await func(**params)
105: 
106:             run.status = "completed"
107:             run.completed_at = datetime.now()
108:             run.metadata["result"] = result
109: 
110:             logger.info(f"Pipeline {pipeline_name} completed (run_id={run_id})")
111: 
112:         except Exception as e:
113:             run.status = "failed"
114:             run.error = str(e)
115:             run.completed_at = datetime.now()
116:             logger.error(f"Pipeline {pipeline_name} failed (run_id={run_id}): {e}")
117:             raise
118: 
119:         return run
120: 
121:     async def run_ingestion(
122:         self,
123:         namespace_id: UUID,
124:         documents: list[dict[str, Any]],
125:         *,
126:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
127:     ) -> PipelineRun:
128:         """Run the standard ingestion pipeline.
129: 
130:         Args:
131:             namespace_id: Namespace to ingest into
132:             documents: List of documents to ingest
133:             skill_name: Extraction skill to use
134: 
135:         Returns:
136:             PipelineRun with execution info
137:         """
138:         return await self.run_pipeline(
139:             "ingest",
140:             namespace_id,
141:             parameters={
142:                 "documents": documents,
143:                 "skill_name": skill_name,
144:             },
145:         )
146: 
147:     async def run_sync(
148:         self,
149:         namespace_id: UUID,
150:         source: str,
151:         *,
152:         connector_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
153:     ) -> PipelineRun:
154:         """Run a sync pipeline for an external source.
155: 
156:         Args:
157:             namespace_id: Namespace to sync into
158:             source: Source name (e.g., "github", "notion")
159:             connector_config: Connector configuration
160: 
161:         Returns:
162:             PipelineRun with execution info
163:         """
164:         return await self.run_pipeline(
165:             f"sync_{source}",
166:             namespace_id,
167:             parameters={
168:                 "source": source,
169:                 "connector_config": connector_config or {},
170:             },
171:         )
172: 
173:     def get_run(self, run_id: str) -> PipelineRun | None:
174:         """Get a pipeline run by ID.
175: 
176:         Args:
177:             run_id: Run ID
178: 
179:         Returns:
180:             PipelineRun or None if not found
181:         """
182:         return self._runs.get(run_id)
183: 
184:     def list_runs(
185:         self,
186:         *,
187:         namespace_id: UUID | None = None,
188:         pipeline_name: str | None = None,
189:         status: str | None = None,
190:     ) -> list[PipelineRun]:
191:         """List pipeline runs with optional filtering.
192: 
193:         Args:
194:             namespace_id: Filter by namespace
195:             pipeline_name: Filter by pipeline name
196:             status: Filter by status
197: 
198:         Returns:
199:             List of matching PipelineRun objects
200:         """
201:         runs = list(self._runs.values())
202: 
203:         if namespace_id:
204:             runs = [r for r in runs if r.namespace_id == namespace_id]
205:         if pipeline_name:
206:             runs = [r for r in runs if r.pipeline_name == pipeline_name]
207:         if status:
208:             runs = [r for r in runs if r.status == status]
209: 
210:         return runs
````

## File: src/khora/pipelines/registry.py
````python
  1: """Pipeline registry with decorators for Khora Memory Lake."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from collections.abc import Callable
  6: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  7: from typing import Any
  8: 
  9: from loguru import logger
 10: 
 11: 
 12: @dataclass
 13: class PipelineInfo:
 14:     """Information about a registered pipeline."""
 15: 
 16:     name: str
 17:     description: str
 18:     func: Callable
 19:     tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 20:     version: str = "1.0.0"
 21:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 22: 
 23: 
 24: class PipelineRegistry:
 25:     """Registry for managing pipeline flows.
 26: 
 27:     Provides registration and discovery of pipeline flows
 28:     that can be run by the PipelineManager.
 29:     """
 30: 
 31:     _instance: PipelineRegistry | None = None
 32:     _pipelines: dict[str, PipelineInfo]
 33: 
 34:     def __new__(cls) -> PipelineRegistry:
 35:         """Singleton pattern for global registry."""
 36:         if cls._instance is None:
 37:             cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
 38:             cls._instance._pipelines = {}
 39:         return cls._instance
 40: 
 41:     def register(
 42:         self,
 43:         name: str,
 44:         *,
 45:         description: str = "",
 46:         tags: list[str] | None = None,
 47:         version: str = "1.0.0",
 48:         **metadata: Any,
 49:     ) -> Callable[[Callable], Callable]:
 50:         """Decorator to register a pipeline flow.
 51: 
 52:         Args:
 53:             name: Pipeline name
 54:             description: Pipeline description
 55:             tags: Pipeline tags for categorization
 56:             version: Pipeline version
 57:             **metadata: Additional metadata
 58: 
 59:         Returns:
 60:             Decorator function
 61:         """
 62: 
 63:         def decorator(func: Callable) -> Callable:
 64:             info = PipelineInfo(
 65:                 name=name,
 66:                 description=description or func.__doc__ or "",
 67:                 func=func,
 68:                 tags=tags or [],
 69:                 version=version,
 70:                 metadata=metadata,
 71:             )
 72:             self._pipelines[name] = info
 73:             logger.debug(f"Registered pipeline: {name}")
 74:             return func
 75: 
 76:         return decorator
 77: 
 78:     def get(self, name: str) -> PipelineInfo | None:
 79:         """Get a pipeline by name.
 80: 
 81:         Args:
 82:             name: Pipeline name
 83: 
 84:         Returns:
 85:             PipelineInfo or None if not found
 86:         """
 87:         return self._pipelines.get(name)
 88: 
 89:     def list_pipelines(self) -> list[str]:
 90:         """List all registered pipeline names.
 91: 
 92:         Returns:
 93:             List of pipeline names
 94:         """
 95:         return list(self._pipelines.keys())
 96: 
 97:     def get_by_tag(self, tag: str) -> list[PipelineInfo]:
 98:         """Get pipelines by tag.
 99: 
100:         Args:
101:             tag: Tag to filter by
102: 
103:         Returns:
104:             List of matching PipelineInfo objects
105:         """
106:         return [p for p in self._pipelines.values() if tag in p.tags]
107: 
108:     def all_pipelines(self) -> list[PipelineInfo]:
109:         """Get all registered pipelines.
110: 
111:         Returns:
112:             List of PipelineInfo objects
113:         """
114:         return list(self._pipelines.values())
115: 
116:     def unregister(self, name: str) -> bool:
117:         """Unregister a pipeline.
118: 
119:         Args:
120:             name: Pipeline name to unregister
121: 
122:         Returns:
123:             True if removed, False if not found
124:         """
125:         if name in self._pipelines:
126:             del self._pipelines[name]
127:             logger.debug(f"Unregistered pipeline: {name}")
128:             return True
129:         return False
130: 
131: 
132: # Global registry instance
133: _registry = PipelineRegistry()
134: 
135: 
136: def pipeline(
137:     name: str,
138:     *,
139:     description: str = "",
140:     tags: list[str] | None = None,
141:     version: str = "1.0.0",
142:     **metadata: Any,
143: ) -> Callable[[Callable], Callable]:
144:     """Decorator to register a pipeline flow.
145: 
146:     Usage:
147:         @pipeline("my_pipeline", description="My pipeline", tags=["ingestion"])
148:         async def my_pipeline(param: str):
149:             ...
150: 
151:     Args:
152:         name: Pipeline name
153:         description: Pipeline description
154:         tags: Pipeline tags
155:         version: Pipeline version
156:         **metadata: Additional metadata
157: 
158:     Returns:
159:         Decorator function
160:     """
161:     return _registry.register(name, description=description, tags=tags, version=version, **metadata)
162: 
163: 
164: def get_registry() -> PipelineRegistry:
165:     """Get the global pipeline registry.
166: 
167:     Returns:
168:         PipelineRegistry instance
169:     """
170:     return _registry
````

## File: src/khora/query/hyde.py
````python
 1: """Hypothetical Document Embeddings (HyDE) for query expansion.
 2: 
 3: Generates a hypothetical answer document using an LLM, embeds it,
 4: and averages with the original query embedding to improve recall
 5: for queries that use different vocabulary than stored documents.
 6: """
 7: 
 8: from __future__ import annotations
 9: 
10: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
11: 
12: import numpy as np
13: from loguru import logger
14: 
15: if TYPE_CHECKING:
16:     from khora.config.llm import LiteLLMConfig
17:     from khora.extraction.embedders import Embedder
18: 
19: 
20: class HyDEExpander:
21:     """Expands query embeddings using Hypothetical Document Embeddings."""
22: 
23:     def __init__(
24:         self,
25:         embedder: Embedder,
26:         llm_config: LiteLLMConfig | None = None,
27:         *,
28:         num_hypotheticals: int = 1,
29:     ) -> None:
30:         self._embedder = embedder
31:         self._llm_config = llm_config
32:         self._num_hypotheticals = num_hypotheticals
33: 
34:     async def generate_hypothetical(self, query: str) -> str:
35:         """Generate a hypothetical document that answers the query."""
36:         from khora.config.llm import acompletion
37: 
38:         system_prompt = (
39:             "You are a knowledgeable assistant. Given a question, write a short, "
40:             "factual passage (2-3 sentences) that directly answers it. "
41:             "Do not include any preamble or meta-commentary, just the answer content."
42:         )
43: 
44:         return await acompletion(
45:             query,
46:             self._llm_config,
47:             system_prompt=system_prompt,
48:             temperature=0.7,
49:             max_tokens=200,
50:         )
51: 
52:     async def expand_query_embedding(
53:         self,
54:         query: str,
55:         query_embedding: list[float],
56:     ) -> list[float]:
57:         """Expand a query embedding by averaging with hypothetical doc embeddings.
58: 
59:         On any failure, returns the original embedding unchanged.
60:         """
61:         from khora.telemetry.instrument import pipeline_stage
62: 
63:         try:
64:             async with pipeline_stage("query", "hyde", extra_metadata={"num_hypotheticals": self._num_hypotheticals}):
65:                 hypotheticals: list[str] = []
66:                 for _ in range(self._num_hypotheticals):
67:                     doc = await self.generate_hypothetical(query)
68:                     hypotheticals.append(doc)
69: 
70:                 # Embed hypothetical documents
71:                 hyde_embeddings: list[list[float]] = []
72:                 for doc in hypotheticals:
73:                     emb = await self._embedder.embed(doc)
74:                     hyde_embeddings.append(emb)
75: 
76:                 # Average: original query embedding + hypothetical embeddings
77:                 all_embeddings = [query_embedding, *hyde_embeddings]
78:                 avg = np.mean(all_embeddings, axis=0)
79:                 return avg.tolist()
80: 
81:         except Exception as e:
82:             logger.warning(f"HyDE expansion failed, using original embedding: {e}")
83:             return query_embedding
````

## File: src/khora/query/keyword.py
````python
  1: """Keyword search module for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Provides BM25-based keyword search for improved recall alongside
  4: vector search.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import math
 10: import re
 11: from collections import Counter
 12: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 13: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
 14: 
 15: from loguru import logger
 16: 
 17: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 18:     from khora.core.models import Chunk
 19: 
 20: 
 21: # Common English stopwords
 22: STOPWORDS = {
 23:     "a",
 24:     "an",
 25:     "and",
 26:     "are",
 27:     "as",
 28:     "at",
 29:     "be",
 30:     "by",
 31:     "for",
 32:     "from",
 33:     "has",
 34:     "he",
 35:     "in",
 36:     "is",
 37:     "it",
 38:     "its",
 39:     "of",
 40:     "on",
 41:     "that",
 42:     "the",
 43:     "to",
 44:     "was",
 45:     "were",
 46:     "will",
 47:     "with",
 48:     "the",
 49:     "this",
 50:     "but",
 51:     "they",
 52:     "have",
 53:     "had",
 54:     "what",
 55:     "when",
 56:     "where",
 57:     "who",
 58:     "which",
 59:     "why",
 60:     "how",
 61:     "all",
 62:     "each",
 63:     "every",
 64:     "both",
 65:     "few",
 66:     "more",
 67:     "most",
 68:     "other",
 69:     "some",
 70:     "such",
 71:     "no",
 72:     "nor",
 73:     "not",
 74:     "only",
 75:     "own",
 76:     "same",
 77:     "so",
 78:     "than",
 79:     "too",
 80:     "very",
 81:     "just",
 82:     "can",
 83:     "should",
 84:     "now",
 85:     "i",
 86:     "you",
 87:     "we",
 88:     "our",
 89:     "your",
 90:     "my",
 91:     "me",
 92:     "him",
 93:     "her",
 94:     "them",
 95:     "their",
 96:     "been",
 97:     "being",
 98:     "do",
 99:     "does",
100:     "did",
101:     "doing",
102:     "would",
103:     "could",
104:     "if",
105:     "then",
106:     "else",
107:     "or",
108:     "because",
109:     "until",
110:     "while",
111:     "am",
112: }
113: 
114: 
115: def tokenize(text: str, use_stemming: bool = True, remove_stopwords: bool = True) -> list[str]:
116:     """Tokenize text for keyword search.
117: 
118:     Args:
119:         text: Text to tokenize
120:         use_stemming: Apply basic stemming
121:         remove_stopwords: Remove common stopwords
122: 
123:     Returns:
124:         List of tokens
125:     """
126:     # Lowercase and split on non-alphanumeric
127:     tokens = re.findall(r"\b[a-zA-Z0-9]+\b", text.lower())
128: 
129:     # Remove stopwords
130:     if remove_stopwords:
131:         tokens = [t for t in tokens if t not in STOPWORDS]
132: 
133:     # Basic stemming (simple suffix removal)
134:     if use_stemming:
135:         tokens = [_basic_stem(t) for t in tokens]
136: 
137:     # Filter short tokens
138:     tokens = [t for t in tokens if len(t) > 2]
139: 
140:     return tokens
141: 
142: 
143: def _basic_stem(word: str) -> str:
144:     """Basic Porter-like stemming.
145: 
146:     Args:
147:         word: Word to stem
148: 
149:     Returns:
150:         Stemmed word
151:     """
152:     # Simple suffix removal
153:     suffixes = ["ing", "ed", "tion", "ness", "ment", "able", "ible", "ful", "less", "ly", "er", "est", "es", "s"]
154: 
155:     for suffix in suffixes:
156:         if word.endswith(suffix) and len(word) > len(suffix) + 2:
157:             return word[: -len(suffix)]
158: 
159:     return word
160: 
161: 
162: @dataclass
163: class BM25Index:
164:     """BM25 index for keyword search.
165: 
166:     Implements the BM25 ranking function for full-text search.
167:     """
168: 
169:     # BM25 parameters
170:     k1: float = 1.5
171:     b: float = 0.75
172: 
173:     # Index data
174:     doc_lengths: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
175:     doc_freqs: dict[str, Counter] = field(default_factory=dict)  # doc_id -> term -> count
176:     term_doc_freqs: Counter = field(default_factory=Counter)  # term -> num_docs
177:     avg_doc_length: float = 0.0
178:     total_docs: int = 0
179: 
180:     # Inverted index: term -> set of doc_ids containing that term
181:     _inverted_index: dict[str, set[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
182: 
183:     # Running sum for O(1) average length updates
184:     _total_length: int = 0
185: 
186:     # Stemming and stopwords
187:     use_stemming: bool = True
188:     remove_stopwords: bool = True
189: 
190:     def add_document(self, doc_id: str, text: str) -> None:
191:         """Add a document to the index.
192: 
193:         Args:
194:             doc_id: Document identifier
195:             text: Document text
196:         """
197:         tokens = tokenize(text, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
198: 
199:         self.doc_lengths[doc_id] = len(tokens)
200:         self.doc_freqs[doc_id] = Counter(tokens)
201: 
202:         # Update term document frequencies and inverted index
203:         for term in set(tokens):
204:             self.term_doc_freqs[term] += 1
205:             if term not in self._inverted_index:
206:                 self._inverted_index[term] = set()
207:             self._inverted_index[term].add(doc_id)
208: 
209:         self.total_docs += 1
210:         self._total_length += len(tokens)
211:         self._update_avg_length()
212: 
213:     def add_documents(self, documents: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> None:
214:         """Add multiple documents to the index.
215: 
216:         Args:
217:             documents: List of (doc_id, text) tuples
218:         """
219:         for doc_id, text in documents:
220:             tokens = tokenize(text, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
221:             self.doc_lengths[doc_id] = len(tokens)
222:             self.doc_freqs[doc_id] = Counter(tokens)
223:             for term in set(tokens):
224:                 self.term_doc_freqs[term] += 1
225:                 if term not in self._inverted_index:
226:                     self._inverted_index[term] = set()
227:                 self._inverted_index[term].add(doc_id)
228:             self.total_docs += 1
229:             self._total_length += len(tokens)
230: 
231:         self._update_avg_length()
232: 
233:     def _update_avg_length(self) -> None:
234:         """Update average document length using running sum (O(1))."""
235:         if self.total_docs > 0:
236:             self.avg_doc_length = self._total_length / self.total_docs
237: 
238:     def _idf(self, term: str) -> float:
239:         """Calculate inverse document frequency for a term.
240: 
241:         Args:
242:             term: Term to calculate IDF for
243: 
244:         Returns:
245:             IDF score
246:         """
247:         n = self.total_docs
248:         df = self.term_doc_freqs.get(term, 0)
249:         if df == 0:
250:             return 0.0
251:         return math.log((n - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1)
252: 
253:     def score(self, query: str, doc_id: str) -> float:
254:         """Calculate BM25 score for a query-document pair.
255: 
256:         Args:
257:             query: Query text
258:             doc_id: Document identifier
259: 
260:         Returns:
261:             BM25 score
262:         """
263:         if doc_id not in self.doc_freqs:
264:             return 0.0
265: 
266:         query_tokens = tokenize(query, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
267:         doc_freq = self.doc_freqs[doc_id]
268:         doc_len = self.doc_lengths[doc_id]
269: 
270:         score = 0.0
271:         for term in query_tokens:
272:             if term not in doc_freq:
273:                 continue
274: 
275:             tf = doc_freq[term]
276:             idf = self._idf(term)
277: 
278:             # BM25 scoring formula
279:             numerator = tf * (self.k1 + 1)
280:             denominator = tf + self.k1 * (1 - self.b + self.b * doc_len / max(self.avg_doc_length, 1))
281:             score += idf * numerator / denominator
282: 
283:         return score
284: 
285:     def search(
286:         self,
287:         query: str,
288:         limit: int = 10,
289:         min_score: float = 0.0,
290:     ) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
291:         """Search the index for relevant documents.
292: 
293:         Uses the inverted index to only score documents containing at
294:         least one query term, reducing complexity from O(D*Q) to
295:         O(postings*Q).
296: 
297:         Args:
298:             query: Query text
299:             limit: Maximum results to return
300:             min_score: Minimum score threshold
301: 
302:         Returns:
303:             List of (doc_id, score) tuples sorted by score
304:         """
305:         query_tokens = tokenize(query, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
306:         if not query_tokens:
307:             return []
308: 
309:         # Gather candidate doc IDs via inverted index
310:         candidate_doc_ids: set[str] = set()
311:         for term in query_tokens:
312:             candidate_doc_ids.update(self._inverted_index.get(term, set()))
313: 
314:         results = []
315:         for doc_id in candidate_doc_ids:
316:             s = self.score(query, doc_id)
317:             if s > min_score:
318:                 results.append((doc_id, s))
319: 
320:         # Sort by score descending
321:         results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
322:         return results[:limit]
323: 
324: 
325: class KeywordSearcher:
326:     """Keyword search for chunks.
327: 
328:     Provides BM25-based search over chunk content.
329:     """
330: 
331:     def __init__(
332:         self,
333:         use_stemming: bool = True,
334:         remove_stopwords: bool = True,
335:         k1: float = 1.5,
336:         b: float = 0.75,
337:     ) -> None:
338:         """Initialize the keyword searcher.
339: 
340:         Args:
341:             use_stemming: Apply stemming
342:             remove_stopwords: Remove stopwords
343:             k1: BM25 k1 parameter
344:             b: BM25 b parameter
345:         """
346:         self._index = BM25Index(
347:             k1=k1,
348:             b=b,
349:             use_stemming=use_stemming,
350:             remove_stopwords=remove_stopwords,
351:         )
352:         self._chunks: dict[str, Chunk] = {}  # doc_id -> Chunk
353: 
354:     def index_chunks(self, chunks: list[Chunk]) -> None:
355:         """Index chunks for keyword search.
356: 
357:         Args:
358:             chunks: Chunks to index
359:         """
360:         for chunk in chunks:
361:             doc_id = str(chunk.id)
362:             self._chunks[doc_id] = chunk
363:             self._index.add_document(doc_id, chunk.content)
364: 
365:         logger.debug(f"Indexed {len(chunks)} chunks for keyword search")
366: 
367:     def search(
368:         self,
369:         query: str,
370:         limit: int = 10,
371:         min_score: float = 0.0,
372:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
373:         """Search for chunks matching the query.
374: 
375:         Args:
376:             query: Query text
377:             limit: Maximum results
378:             min_score: Minimum BM25 score
379: 
380:         Returns:
381:             List of (chunk, score) tuples
382:         """
383:         results = self._index.search(query, limit=limit, min_score=min_score)
384: 
385:         chunk_results = []
386:         for doc_id, score in results:
387:             if doc_id in self._chunks:
388:                 chunk_results.append((self._chunks[doc_id], score))
389: 
390:         return chunk_results
391: 
392:     def search_with_keywords(
393:         self,
394:         keywords: list[str],
395:         limit: int = 10,
396:         min_score: float = 0.0,
397:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
398:         """Search using pre-extracted keywords.
399: 
400:         Args:
401:             keywords: Keywords to search for
402:             limit: Maximum results
403:             min_score: Minimum BM25 score
404: 
405:         Returns:
406:             List of (chunk, score) tuples
407:         """
408:         # Join keywords into query
409:         query = " ".join(keywords)
410:         return self.search(query, limit=limit, min_score=min_score)
411: 
412: 
413: async def build_keyword_index(
414:     chunks: list[Chunk],
415:     use_stemming: bool = True,
416:     remove_stopwords: bool = True,
417: ) -> KeywordSearcher:
418:     """Build a keyword search index from chunks.
419: 
420:     Args:
421:         chunks: Chunks to index
422:         use_stemming: Apply stemming
423:         remove_stopwords: Remove stopwords
424: 
425:     Returns:
426:         KeywordSearcher instance
427:     """
428:     searcher = KeywordSearcher(
429:         use_stemming=use_stemming,
430:         remove_stopwords=remove_stopwords,
431:     )
432:     searcher.index_chunks(chunks)
433:     return searcher
434: 
435: 
436: def normalize_bm25_score(score: float, max_score: float = 10.0) -> float:
437:     """Normalize BM25 score to 0-1 range.
438: 
439:     Uses sigmoid-like normalization.
440: 
441:     Args:
442:         score: Raw BM25 score
443:         max_score: Expected max score for normalization
444: 
445:     Returns:
446:         Normalized score between 0 and 1
447:     """
448:     if score <= 0:
449:         return 0.0
450:     return min(1.0, score / max_score)
````

## File: src/khora/query/message_extract.py
````python
 1: """Utilities for extracting individual messages from conversation chunks.
 2: 
 3: Conversation chunks produced by :class:`ConversationChunker` embed
 4: per-message metadata (author, timestamp, character offsets) so that
 5: individual messages can be retrieved from search results.
 6: """
 7: 
 8: from __future__ import annotations
 9: 
10: from typing import Any
11: 
12: 
13: def extract_messages_from_chunk(chunk_metadata: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
14:     """Return the per-message metadata list from a chunk.
15: 
16:     Args:
17:         chunk_metadata: The ``metadata`` dict of a chunk or search result.
18: 
19:     Returns:
20:         List of message dicts (each with ``id``, ``author``, ``timestamp``,
21:         ``start_char``, ``end_char``).  Returns an empty list when the
22:         metadata does not contain conversation message data.
23:     """
24:     return chunk_metadata.get("messages", [])
25: 
26: 
27: def extract_message_text(chunk_content: str, start_char: int, end_char: int) -> str:
28:     """Slice a message's text from the chunk content using character offsets.
29: 
30:     Args:
31:         chunk_content: The full chunk text.
32:         start_char: Start character offset (inclusive).
33:         end_char: End character offset (exclusive).
34: 
35:     Returns:
36:         The substring corresponding to the message.
37:     """
38:     return chunk_content[start_char:end_char]
39: 
40: 
41: def find_message_in_chunk(
42:     chunk_content: str,
43:     chunk_metadata: dict[str, Any],
44:     message_id: str,
45: ) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
46:     """Find a specific message by ID within a conversation chunk.
47: 
48:     Args:
49:         chunk_content: The full chunk text.
50:         chunk_metadata: The chunk's metadata dict.
51:         message_id: The ``message_id`` to look up.
52: 
53:     Returns:
54:         A dict with ``id``, ``author``, ``timestamp``, and ``text`` keys,
55:         or ``None`` if the message was not found.
56:     """
57:     for msg in extract_messages_from_chunk(chunk_metadata):
58:         if msg.get("id") == message_id:
59:             text = extract_message_text(chunk_content, msg["start_char"], msg["end_char"])
60:             return {
61:                 "id": msg["id"],
62:                 "author": msg["author"],
63:                 "timestamp": msg["timestamp"],
64:                 "text": text,
65:             }
66:     return None
````

## File: src/khora/storage/backends/__init__.py
````python
 1: """Storage backend implementations for Khora Memory Lake."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from .base import (
 6:     EventStoreProtocol,
 7:     GraphBackendProtocol,
 8:     RelationalBackendProtocol,
 9:     VectorBackendProtocol,
10: )
11: from .mixins import GraphBackendBase, VectorBackendBase
12: from .neo4j import Neo4jBackend
13: from .pgvector import PgVectorBackend
14: from .postgresql import PostgreSQLBackend
15: 
16: # Lazy imports for optional backends
17: try:
18:     from .kuzu import KuzuBackend
19: except ImportError:
20:     KuzuBackend = None  # type: ignore[assignment,misc]
21: 
22: try:
23:     from .memgraph import MemgraphBackend
24: except ImportError:
25:     MemgraphBackend = None  # type: ignore[assignment,misc]
26: 
27: try:
28:     from .arcadedb import ArcadeDBBackend
29: except ImportError:
30:     ArcadeDBBackend = None  # type: ignore[assignment,misc]
31: 
32: __all__ = [
33:     # Protocols
34:     "RelationalBackendProtocol",
35:     "VectorBackendProtocol",
36:     "GraphBackendProtocol",
37:     "EventStoreProtocol",
38:     # Mixins
39:     "GraphBackendBase",
40:     "VectorBackendBase",
41:     # Implementations
42:     "PostgreSQLBackend",
43:     "PgVectorBackend",
44:     "Neo4jBackend",
45:     "KuzuBackend",
46:     "MemgraphBackend",
47:     "ArcadeDBBackend",
48: ]
````

## File: src/khora/storage/backends/mixins.py
````python
  1: """Mixin base classes for storage backends.
  2: 
  3: Provide default implementations for batch/aggregate operations so new
  4: backends get them for free and can override with optimized versions.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import json
 10: from datetime import datetime
 11: from typing import Any
 12: from uuid import UUID
 13: 
 14: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 15: # Serialization helpers (shared across graph backends)
 16: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 17: 
 18: 
 19: def serialize_dict(value: dict[str, Any] | None) -> str | None:
 20:     """Serialize a dict to JSON string for property-store backends.
 21: 
 22:     Graph databases typically only support primitive property values;
 23:     nested dicts must be stored as JSON strings.
 24:     """
 25:     if value is None:
 26:         return None
 27:     return json.dumps(value)
 28: 
 29: 
 30: def deserialize_dict(value: str | dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
 31:     """Deserialize a JSON string back to dict.
 32: 
 33:     Handles both string (serialized) and dict (legacy/native) values.
 34:     """
 35:     if value is None:
 36:         return {}
 37:     if isinstance(value, dict):
 38:         return value
 39:     try:
 40:         return json.loads(value)
 41:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
 42:         return {}
 43: 
 44: 
 45: def element_to_dict(element: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
 46:     """Safely convert a graph element (Node, Relationship, or raw value) to a dict.
 47: 
 48:     Handles Neo4j Node/Relationship objects, Kùzu results, plain dicts, etc.
 49:     """
 50:     if isinstance(element, dict):
 51:         return element
 52:     # neo4j.graph.Node / Relationship expose _properties
 53:     if hasattr(element, "_properties"):
 54:         return dict(element._properties)
 55:     # Some driver versions make Node/Relationship a Mapping
 56:     if hasattr(element, "items"):
 57:         try:
 58:             return dict(element.items())
 59:         except Exception:
 60:             pass
 61:     # Last resort — avoid crashing on unexpected types
 62:     return {"_raw": str(element)}
 63: 
 64: 
 65: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 66: # Parse helpers for record→domain model conversions
 67: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 68: 
 69: 
 70: def parse_uuid(val: Any) -> UUID:
 71:     """Parse a UUID from a string or UUID."""
 72:     if isinstance(val, UUID):
 73:         return val
 74:     return UUID(str(val))
 75: 
 76: 
 77: def parse_uuid_list(val: list | None) -> list[UUID]:
 78:     """Parse a list of UUIDs."""
 79:     if not val:
 80:         return []
 81:     return [parse_uuid(v) for v in val]
 82: 
 83: 
 84: def parse_datetime(val: str | datetime | None, default: datetime | None = None) -> datetime | None:
 85:     """Parse an ISO datetime string or pass through a datetime."""
 86:     if val is None:
 87:         return default
 88:     if isinstance(val, datetime):
 89:         return val
 90:     return datetime.fromisoformat(val)
 91: 
 92: 
 93: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 94: # GraphBackendBase
 95: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 96: 
 97: 
 98: class GraphBackendBase:
 99:     """Mixin providing default N+1 implementations for batch/aggregate graph ops.
100: 
101:     Concrete graph backends inherit this alongside their protocol implementation.
102:     Backends that support native batch queries should override these methods.
103:     """
104: 
105:     async def get_entities_batch(self, entity_ids: list[UUID]) -> dict[UUID, Entity]:  # noqa: F821
106:         """Default N+1 implementation — subclasses should override for efficiency."""
107:         if not entity_ids:
108:             return {}
109:         result: dict[UUID, Any] = {}
110:         for eid in entity_ids:
111:             entity = await self.get_entity(eid)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
112:             if entity is not None:
113:                 result[eid] = entity
114:         return result
115: 
116:     async def get_neighborhoods_batch(
117:         self,
118:         entity_ids: list[UUID],
119:         *,
120:         depth: int = 1,
121:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
122:         limit_per_entity: int = 20,
123:     ) -> dict[UUID, dict[str, Any]]:
124:         """Default N+1 implementation — subclasses should override for efficiency."""
125:         if not entity_ids:
126:             return {}
127:         result: dict[UUID, dict[str, Any]] = {}
128:         for eid in entity_ids:
129:             neighborhood = await self.get_neighborhood(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
130:                 eid,
131:                 depth=depth,
132:                 relationship_types=relationship_types,
133:                 limit=limit_per_entity,
134:             )
135:             result[eid] = neighborhood
136:         return result
137: 
138:     async def count_entities(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
139:         """Default implementation using list_entities — subclasses should override."""
140:         entities = await self.list_entities(namespace_id, limit=100_000)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
141:         return len(entities)
142: 
143: 
144: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145: # VectorBackendBase
146: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147: 
148: 
149: class VectorBackendBase:
150:     """Mixin providing default implementations for aggregate vector ops.
151: 
152:     Concrete vector backends inherit this alongside their protocol implementation.
153:     """
154: 
155:     async def count_chunks(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
156:         """Default implementation — subclasses should override for efficiency."""
157:         # Fallback: this is intentionally inefficient; override in real backends
158:         return 0
159: 
160:     async def list_chunks(
161:         self,
162:         namespace_id: UUID,
163:         *,
164:         limit: int = 100,
165:         offset: int = 0,
166:     ) -> list:
167:         """Default implementation — subclasses should override."""
168:         return []
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/config.py
````python
 1: """Telemetry configuration."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: import os
 6: 
 7: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
 8: 
 9: 
10: class TelemetryConfig(BaseModel):
11:     """Configuration for the telemetry subsystem."""
12: 
13:     database_url: str | None = Field(
14:         default=None,
15:         description="PostgreSQL URL for telemetry database",
16:     )
17:     service_name: str = Field(
18:         default="khora",
19:         description="Service name tag for telemetry events",
20:     )
21:     flush_interval_seconds: float = Field(default=5.0, description="Seconds between background flushes")
22:     flush_threshold: int = Field(default=100, description="Buffer size that triggers an immediate flush")
23: 
24:     @classmethod
25:     def from_env(cls) -> TelemetryConfig:
26:         """Build config from environment variables."""
27:         return cls(
28:             database_url=os.getenv("KHORA_TELEMETRY_DATABASE_URL"),
29:             service_name=os.getenv("KHORA_TELEMETRY_SERVICE_NAME", "khora"),
30:         )
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/context.py
````python
 1: """Trace propagation via contextvars.
 2: 
 3: Provides request-level trace_id and parent_event_id propagation through
 4: the async call stack without threading parameters through every function.
 5: 
 6: Usage::
 7: 
 8:     from khora.telemetry.context import set_trace_id, ensure_trace_id
 9: 
10:     # At request entry point (remember/recall/chat):
11:     set_trace_id(uuid4())
12: 
13:     # Downstream code automatically inherits trace_id:
14:     trace_id = get_trace_id()  # returns the UUID set above
15: """
16: 
17: from __future__ import annotations
18: 
19: from contextvars import ContextVar
20: from uuid import UUID, uuid4
21: 
22: _trace_id_var: ContextVar[UUID | None] = ContextVar("khora_trace_id", default=None)
23: _parent_event_id_var: ContextVar[int | None] = ContextVar("khora_parent_event_id", default=None)
24: 
25: 
26: def get_trace_id() -> UUID | None:
27:     """Get the current trace ID, or None if not set."""
28:     return _trace_id_var.get()
29: 
30: 
31: def set_trace_id(trace_id: UUID | None) -> None:
32:     """Set the trace ID for the current async context."""
33:     _trace_id_var.set(trace_id)
34: 
35: 
36: def ensure_trace_id() -> UUID:
37:     """Get or create a trace ID for the current context."""
38:     tid = _trace_id_var.get()
39:     if tid is None:
40:         tid = uuid4()
41:         _trace_id_var.set(tid)
42:     return tid
43: 
44: 
45: def clear_trace_id() -> None:
46:     """Clear the trace ID (call at end of request)."""
47:     _trace_id_var.set(None)
48: 
49: 
50: def get_parent_event_id() -> int | None:
51:     """Get the current parent event ID, or None."""
52:     return _parent_event_id_var.get()
53: 
54: 
55: def set_parent_event_id(event_id: int | None) -> None:
56:     """Set the parent event ID for child event linking."""
57:     _parent_event_id_var.set(event_id)
58: 
59: 
60: def clear_parent_event_id() -> None:
61:     """Clear the parent event ID."""
62:     _parent_event_id_var.set(None)
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/noop.py

````python
 1: """No-op telemetry collector -- zero overhead when telemetry is disabled."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from typing import Any
 6: 
 7: 
 8: class NoOpCollector:
 9:     """Drop-in replacement for TelemetryCollector that does nothing."""
10: 
11:     async def start(self) -> None:
12:         pass
13: 
14:     async def shutdown(self) -> None:
15:         pass
16: 
17:     def record_llm_call(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
18:         pass
19: 
20:     def record_storage_op(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
21:         pass
22: 
23:     def record_pipeline_stage(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
24:         pass
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/session.py
````python
 1: """Separate async engine for the telemetry database."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncEngine, create_async_engine
 6: 
 7: 
 8: def create_telemetry_engine(database_url: str) -> AsyncEngine:
 9:     """Create an async engine for the telemetry database.
10: 
11:     The engine is completely independent from khora's main DB engine.
12: 
13:     Args:
14:         database_url: PostgreSQL connection URL.
15: 
16:     Returns:
17:         AsyncEngine configured for telemetry writes.
18:     """
19:     url = database_url
20:     if url.startswith("postgresql://"):
21:         url = url.replace("postgresql://", "postgresql+asyncpg://", 1)
22:     elif url.startswith("postgres://"):
23:         url = url.replace("postgres://", "postgresql+asyncpg://", 1)
24: 
25:     return create_async_engine(
26:         url,
27:         echo=False,
28:         pool_size=5,
29:         max_overflow=5,
30:         pool_pre_ping=True,
31:     )
````

````python
 1: """Unit tests for temporal database models."""
 2: 
 3: from datetime import UTC, datetime
 4: from uuid import uuid4
 5: 
 6: from khora.db.models import (
 7:     TemporalEdgeModel,
 8:     TimeEdgeLinkModel,
 9:     TimeGranularity,
10:     TimeNodeModel,
11: )

13: 
14: class TestTimeGranularityConstants:
15:     """Test TimeGranularity class constants."""

17:     def test_year(self):
18:         assert TimeGranularity.YEAR == "year"
19: 
20:     def test_quarter(self):
21:         assert TimeGranularity.QUARTER == "quarter"
22: 
23:     def test_month(self):
24:         assert TimeGranularity.MONTH == "month"
25: 
26:     def test_week(self):
27:         assert TimeGranularity.WEEK == "week"
28: 
29:     def test_day(self):
30:         assert TimeGranularity.DAY == "day"
31:

33: class TestTimeNodeModel:
34:     """Tests for TimeNodeModel."""
35: 
36:     def test_repr(self):
37:         """Test string representation."""
38:         node = TimeNodeModel(
39:             id=str(uuid4()),
40:             namespace_id=str(uuid4()),
41:             granularity=TimeGranularity.MONTH,
42:             start_time=datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
43:             end_time=datetime(2024, 2, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
44:             name="January 2024",
45:         )
46:         repr_str = repr(node)
47:         assert "TimeNode" in repr_str
48:         assert "January 2024" in repr_str
49:         assert "month" in repr_str
50:

52: class TestTemporalEdgeModel:
53:     """Tests for TemporalEdgeModel."""

55:     def test_repr(self):
56:         """Test string representation."""
57:         now = datetime.now(UTC)
58:         edge = TemporalEdgeModel(
59:             id=str(uuid4()),
60:             namespace_id=str(uuid4()),
61:             source_entity_id=str(uuid4()),
62:             target_entity_id=str(uuid4()),
63:             relationship_type="WORKS_FOR",
64:             occurred_at=now,
65:         )
66:         repr_str = repr(edge)
67:         assert "TemporalEdge" in repr_str
68:         assert "WORKS_FOR" in repr_str

80:             edge_id=edge_id,
81:         )
82:         repr_str = repr(link)
83:         assert "TimeEdgeLink" in repr_str
84:         assert time_node_id in repr_str
85:         assert edge_id in repr_str
````
