````python
288:     async def update_namespace(self, namespace: MemoryNamespace) -> MemoryNamespace:
289:         """Update a namespace."""
290:         async with self._get_session() as session:
291:             await session.execute(
292:                 update(MemoryNamespaceModel)
293:                 .where(MemoryNamespaceModel.id == str(namespace.id))
294:                 .values(
295:                     name=namespace.name,
296:                     slug=namespace.slug,
297:                     description=namespace.description,
298:                     version=namespace.version,
299:                     is_active=namespace.is_active,
300:                     previous_version_id=str(namespace.previous_version_id) if namespace.previous_version_id else None,
301:                     config_overrides=namespace.config_overrides,
302:                     sync_checkpoints=namespace.sync_checkpoints,
303:                     metadata_=namespace.metadata,
304:                     updated_at=datetime.now(UTC),
305:                 )
306:             )
307:             await session.commit()
308:             return namespace
309: 
310:     def _namespace_model_to_domain(self, model: MemoryNamespaceModel) -> MemoryNamespace:
311:         """Convert MemoryNamespaceModel to domain MemoryNamespace."""
312:         return MemoryNamespace(
313:             id=UUID(model.id),
314:             workspace_id=UUID(model.workspace_id),
315:             name=model.name,
316:             slug=model.slug,
317:             description=model.description,
318:             version=model.version,
319:             is_active=model.is_active,
320:             previous_version_id=UUID(model.previous_version_id) if model.previous_version_id else None,
321:             config_overrides=model.config_overrides,
322:             sync_checkpoints=model.sync_checkpoints,
323:             metadata=model.metadata_,
324:             created_at=model.created_at,
325:             updated_at=model.updated_at,
326:         )
327: 
328:     async def create_namespace_version(
329:         self,
330:         workspace_id: UUID,
331:         slug: str,
332:         *,
333:         previous_version: MemoryNamespace | None = None,
334:     ) -> MemoryNamespace:
335:         """Create a new version of a namespace.
336: 
337:         If previous_version is provided, increments its version number and links to it.
338:         The previous version is marked as inactive.
339: 
340:         Args:
341:             workspace_id: Workspace ID
342:             slug: Namespace slug
343:             previous_version: The previous version to supersede (if any)
344: 
345:         Returns:
346:             New namespace version
347:         """
348:         from uuid import uuid4
349: 
350:         new_version = 1
351:         previous_id = None
352: 
353:         if previous_version:
354:             new_version = previous_version.version + 1
355:             previous_id = previous_version.id
356:             # Deactivate the old version
357:             await self.deactivate_namespace(previous_version.id)
358: 
359:         # Create new namespace with incremented version
360:         namespace = MemoryNamespace(
361:             id=uuid4(),
362:             workspace_id=workspace_id,
363:             name=previous_version.name if previous_version else f"Namespace {slug}",
364:             slug=slug,
365:             description=previous_version.description if previous_version else "",
366:             version=new_version,
367:             is_active=True,
368:             previous_version_id=previous_id,
369:             config_overrides=previous_version.config_overrides if previous_version else {},
370:             metadata=previous_version.metadata if previous_version else {},
371:         )
372: 
373:         return await self.create_namespace(namespace)
374: 
375:     async def deactivate_namespace(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> None:
376:         """Mark a namespace version as inactive.
377: 
378:         Args:
379:             namespace_id: ID of the namespace to deactivate
380:         """
381:         async with self._get_session() as session:
382:             await session.execute(
383:                 update(MemoryNamespaceModel)
384:                 .where(MemoryNamespaceModel.id == str(namespace_id))
385:                 .values(is_active=False, updated_at=datetime.now(UTC))
386:             )
387:             await session.commit()
388:             logger.info(f"Deactivated namespace {namespace_id}")
389: 
390:     # =========================================================================
391:     # Document operations
392:     # =========================================================================
393: 
394:     async def create_document(self, document: Document) -> Document:
395:         """Create a new document."""
396:         async with self._get_session() as session:
397:             model = DocumentModel(
398:                 id=str(document.id),
399:                 namespace_id=str(document.namespace_id),
400:                 content=document.content,
401:                 status=document.status,
402:                 source=document.metadata.source,
403:                 source_type=document.metadata.source_type,
404:                 content_type=document.metadata.content_type,
405:                 title=document.metadata.title,
406:                 author=document.metadata.author,
407:                 language=document.metadata.language,
408:                 checksum=document.metadata.checksum,
409:                 size_bytes=document.metadata.size_bytes,
410:                 metadata_=document.metadata.custom,
411:                 chunk_count=document.chunk_count,
412:                 entity_count=document.entity_count,
413:                 error_message=document.error_message,
414:                 created_at=document.created_at,
415:                 updated_at=document.updated_at,
416:                 processed_at=document.processed_at,
417:             )
418:             session.add(model)
419:             await session.commit()
420:             await session.refresh(model)
421:             return self._document_model_to_domain(model)
422: 
423:     async def get_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> Document | None:
424:         """Get a document by ID."""
425:         async with self._get_session() as session:
426:             result = await session.execute(select(DocumentModel).where(DocumentModel.id == str(document_id)))
427:             model = result.scalar_one_or_none()
428:             return self._document_model_to_domain(model) if model else None
429: 
430:     async def list_documents(
431:         self,
432:         namespace_id: UUID,
433:         *,
434:         status: str | None = None,
435:         limit: int = 100,
436:         offset: int = 0,
437:     ) -> list[Document]:
438:         """List documents in a namespace."""
439:         async with self._get_session() as session:
440:             query = select(DocumentModel).where(DocumentModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id))
441:             if status:
442:                 query = query.where(DocumentModel.status == status)
443:             query = query.limit(limit).offset(offset).order_by(DocumentModel.created_at.desc())
444:             result = await session.execute(query)
445:             return [self._document_model_to_domain(m) for m in result.scalars().all()]
446: 
447:     async def update_document(self, document: Document) -> Document:
448:         """Update a document."""
449:         async with self._get_session() as session:
450:             await session.execute(
451:                 update(DocumentModel)
452:                 .where(DocumentModel.id == str(document.id))
453:                 .values(
454:                     content=document.content,
455:                     status=document.status,
456:                     source=document.metadata.source,
457:                     source_type=document.metadata.source_type,
458:                     content_type=document.metadata.content_type,
459:                     title=document.metadata.title,
460:                     author=document.metadata.author,
461:                     language=document.metadata.language,
462:                     checksum=document.metadata.checksum,
463:                     size_bytes=document.metadata.size_bytes,
464:                     metadata_=document.metadata.custom,
465:                     chunk_count=document.chunk_count,
466:                     entity_count=document.entity_count,
467:                     error_message=document.error_message,
468:                     updated_at=datetime.now(UTC),
469:                     processed_at=document.processed_at,
470:                 )
471:             )
472:             await session.commit()
473:             return document
474: 
475:     async def delete_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> bool:
476:         """Delete a document."""
477:         async with self._get_session() as session:
478:             result = await session.execute(select(DocumentModel).where(DocumentModel.id == str(document_id)))
479:             model = result.scalar_one_or_none()
480:             if model:
481:                 await session.delete(model)
482:                 await session.commit()
483:                 return True
484:             return False
485: 
486:     async def get_document_by_checksum(self, namespace_id: UUID, checksum: str) -> Document | None:
487:         """Get a document by its content checksum (for deduplication).
488: 
489:         Returns the first matching document if multiple exist with the same checksum.
490:         """
491:         async with self._get_session() as session:
492:             result = await session.execute(
493:                 select(DocumentModel).where(
494:                     DocumentModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id), DocumentModel.checksum == checksum
495:                 )
496:             )
497:             model = result.scalars().first()
498:             return self._document_model_to_domain(model) if model else None
499: 
500:     async def get_documents_batch(self, document_ids: list[UUID]) -> dict[UUID, Document]:
501:         """Fetch multiple documents in a single query.
502: 
503:         Args:
504:             document_ids: List of document IDs to fetch
505: 
506:         Returns:
507:             Dictionary mapping document ID to Document object
508:         """
509:         if not document_ids:
510:             return {}
511: 
512:         async with self._get_session() as session:
513:             result = await session.execute(
514:                 select(DocumentModel).where(DocumentModel.id.in_([str(did) for did in document_ids]))
515:             )
516:             models = result.scalars().all()
517:             return {UUID(m.id): self._document_model_to_domain(m) for m in models}
518: 
519:     async def get_documents_by_checksums(self, namespace_id: UUID, checksums: list[str]) -> dict[str, Document]:
520:         """Fetch documents by content checksums in a single query.
521: 
522:         Used for batch deduplication to avoid N serial DB queries.
523: 
524:         Args:
525:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
526:             checksums: List of content checksums to look up
527: 
528:         Returns:
529:             Dictionary mapping checksum to Document (only for existing documents)
530:         """
531:         if not checksums:
532:             return {}
533: 
534:         async with self._get_session() as session:
535:             result = await session.execute(
536:                 select(DocumentModel).where(
537:                     DocumentModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id),
538:                     DocumentModel.checksum.in_(checksums),
539:                 )
540:             )
541:             models = result.scalars().all()
542:             return {m.checksum: self._document_model_to_domain(m) for m in models}
543: 
544:     def _document_model_to_domain(self, model: DocumentModel) -> Document:
545:         """Convert DocumentModel to domain Document."""
546:         return Document(
547:             id=UUID(model.id),
548:             namespace_id=UUID(model.namespace_id),
549:             content=model.content,
550:             status=DocumentStatus(model.status) if isinstance(model.status, str) else model.status,
551:             metadata=DocumentMetadata(
552:                 source=model.source,
553:                 source_type=model.source_type,
554:                 content_type=model.content_type,
555:                 title=model.title,
556:                 author=model.author,
557:                 language=model.language,
558:                 checksum=model.checksum,
559:                 size_bytes=model.size_bytes,
560:                 custom=model.metadata_,
561:             ),
562:             chunk_count=model.chunk_count,
563:             entity_count=model.entity_count,
564:             error_message=model.error_message,
565:             created_at=model.created_at,
566:             updated_at=model.updated_at,
567:             processed_at=model.processed_at,
568:         )
569: 
570:     # =========================================================================
571:     # Sync checkpoint operations
572:     # =========================================================================
573: 
574:     async def get_sync_checkpoint(self, namespace_id: UUID, source: str) -> str | None:
575:         """Get the last sync checkpoint for a source."""
576:         async with self._get_session() as session:
577:             result = await session.execute(
578:                 select(SyncCheckpointModel).where(
579:                     SyncCheckpointModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id), SyncCheckpointModel.source == source
580:                 )
581:             )
582:             model = result.scalar_one_or_none()
583:             return model.checkpoint if model else None
584: 
585:     async def set_sync_checkpoint(self, namespace_id: UUID, source: str, checkpoint: str) -> None:
586:         """Set the sync checkpoint for a source."""
587:         async with self._get_session() as session:
588:             result = await session.execute(
589:                 select(SyncCheckpointModel).where(
590:                     SyncCheckpointModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id), SyncCheckpointModel.source == source
591:                 )
592:             )
593:             model = result.scalar_one_or_none()
594:             if model:
595:                 model.checkpoint = checkpoint
596:                 model.updated_at = datetime.now(UTC)
597:             else:
598:                 model = SyncCheckpointModel(
599:                     namespace_id=str(namespace_id),
600:                     source=source,
601:                     checkpoint=checkpoint,
602:                 )
603:                 session.add(model)
604:             await session.commit()
````

## File: src/khora/storage/__init__.py
````python
 1: """Storage layer for Khora Memory Lake.
 2: 
 3: Provides unified access to multiple storage backends:
 4: - PostgreSQL: Relational data (documents, events, tenancy, ACLs)
 5: - pgvector: Vector embeddings for semantic search
 6: - Neo4j: Knowledge graph (entities, relationships)
 7: - Kùzu: Embedded graph database (optional)
 8: - Memgraph: In-memory graph database (optional)
 9: - ArcadeDB: Multi-model graph + vector database (optional)
10: """
11: 
12: from __future__ import annotations
13: 
14: from .backends.base import (
15:     EventStoreProtocol,
16:     GraphBackendProtocol,
17:     RelationalBackendProtocol,
18:     VectorBackendProtocol,
19: )
20: from .backends.neo4j import Neo4jBackend
21: from .backends.pgvector import PgVectorBackend
22: from .backends.postgresql import PostgreSQLBackend
23: from .coordinator import StorageCoordinator
24: from .event_store import PostgreSQLEventStore
25: from .expertise_store import ExpertiseStore
26: from .factory import StorageConfig, StorageFactory, create_storage_coordinator
27: from .optimize import optimize_neo4j, optimize_postgresql, optimize_storage
28: 
29: __all__ = [
30:     # Protocols
31:     "RelationalBackendProtocol",
32:     "VectorBackendProtocol",
33:     "GraphBackendProtocol",
34:     "EventStoreProtocol",
35:     # Implementations
36:     "PostgreSQLBackend",
37:     "PgVectorBackend",
38:     "Neo4jBackend",
39:     "PostgreSQLEventStore",
40:     # Coordinator
41:     "StorageCoordinator",
42:     "StorageConfig",
43:     "StorageFactory",
44:     "create_storage_coordinator",
45:     # Optimization
46:     "optimize_storage",
47:     "optimize_postgresql",
48:     "optimize_neo4j",
49:     # Expertise
50:     "ExpertiseStore",
51: ]
````

## File: src/khora/storage/factory.py
````python
  1: """Storage factory for creating storage backends and coordinator.
  2: 
  3: Creates and configures storage backends based on configuration.
  4: Supports registry-based dispatch for multiple graph and vector backend types.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import importlib
 10: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 11: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 12: 
 13: from loguru import logger
 14: 
 15: from .backends.pgvector import PgVectorBackend
 16: from .backends.postgresql import PostgreSQLBackend
 17: from .coordinator import StorageCoordinator
 18: 
 19: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 20:     from .backends.base import EventStoreProtocol, GraphBackendProtocol, VectorBackendProtocol
 21: 
 22: 
 23: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 24: # Backend registries: backend_name → (module_path, class_name)
 25: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 26: 
 27: _GRAPH_REGISTRY: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
 28:     "neo4j": ("khora.storage.backends.neo4j", "Neo4jBackend"),
 29:     "kuzu": ("khora.storage.backends.kuzu", "KuzuBackend"),
 30:     "memgraph": ("khora.storage.backends.memgraph", "MemgraphBackend"),
 31:     "arcadedb": ("khora.storage.backends.arcadedb", "ArcadeDBBackend"),
 32: }
 33: 
 34: _VECTOR_REGISTRY: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
 35:     "pgvector": ("khora.storage.backends.pgvector", "PgVectorBackend"),
 36:     "arcadedb": ("khora.storage.backends.arcadedb", "ArcadeDBBackend"),
 37: }
 38: 
 39: 
 40: def _import_backend_class(module_path: str, class_name: str) -> type | None:
 41:     """Lazily import a backend class. Returns None if the dependency is missing."""
 42:     try:
 43:         module = importlib.import_module(module_path)
 44:         return getattr(module, class_name)
 45:     except ImportError as e:
 46:         logger.warning(f"Cannot import {class_name} from {module_path}: {e}")
 47:         return None
 48:     except AttributeError:
 49:         logger.warning(f"Class {class_name} not found in {module_path}")
 50:         return None
 51: 
 52: 
 53: @dataclass
 54: class StorageConfig:
 55:     """Configuration for storage backends.
 56: 
 57:     Supports both legacy flat fields (neo4j_url, pgvector_url, etc.) and
 58:     new-style discriminated union configs (graph_config, vector_config).
 59:     """
 60: 
 61:     # PostgreSQL configuration
 62:     postgresql_url: str | None = None
 63:     postgresql_echo: bool = False
 64:     postgresql_pool_size: int = 10
 65:     postgresql_max_overflow: int = 20
 66: 
 67:     # pgvector configuration (can share PostgreSQL URL) — legacy
 68:     pgvector_url: str | None = None
 69:     pgvector_embedding_dimension: int = 1536
 70: 
 71:     # Neo4j configuration — legacy
 72:     neo4j_url: str | None = None
 73:     neo4j_user: str = "neo4j"
 74:     neo4j_password: str = ""
 75:     neo4j_database: str = "neo4j"
 76: 
 77:     # New-style backend configs (Pydantic models from config/schema.py)
 78:     graph_config: Any = None  # GraphConfig union type
 79:     vector_config: Any = None  # VectorConfig union type
 80: 
 81:     # Event store configuration (uses PostgreSQL by default)
 82:     event_store_url: str | None = None
 83: 
 84:     @classmethod
 85:     def from_dict(cls, config: dict[str, Any]) -> StorageConfig:
 86:         """Create configuration from a dictionary."""
 87:         storage_config = config.get("storage", {})
 88: 
 89:         # Extract relational config
 90:         relational = storage_config.get("relational", {})
 91:         postgresql_url = relational.get("url") or config.get("database_url")
 92: 
 93:         # Extract vector config
 94:         vector = storage_config.get("vector", {})
 95:         pgvector_url = vector.get("url") or postgresql_url  # Default to same as PostgreSQL
 96:         embedding_dimension = vector.get("embedding_dimension", 1536)
 97: 
 98:         # Extract graph config
 99:         graph = storage_config.get("graph", {})
100:         neo4j_url = graph.get("url")
101:         neo4j_user = graph.get("user", "neo4j")
102:         neo4j_password = graph.get("password", "")
103:         neo4j_database = graph.get("database", "neo4j")
104: 
105:         return cls(
106:             postgresql_url=postgresql_url,
107:             postgresql_echo=relational.get("echo", False),
108:             postgresql_pool_size=relational.get("pool_size", 5),
109:             postgresql_max_overflow=relational.get("max_overflow", 10),
110:             pgvector_url=pgvector_url,
111:             pgvector_embedding_dimension=embedding_dimension,
112:             neo4j_url=neo4j_url,
113:             neo4j_user=neo4j_user,
114:             neo4j_password=neo4j_password,
115:             neo4j_database=neo4j_database,
116:             event_store_url=storage_config.get("event_store", {}).get("url") or postgresql_url,
117:         )
118: 
119: 
120: # Cache for ArcadeDB dual-role instance sharing
121: _arcadedb_instances: dict[str, Any] = {}
122: 
123: 
124: @dataclass
125: class StorageFactory:
126:     """Factory for creating storage backends."""
127: 
128:     config: StorageConfig = field(default_factory=StorageConfig)
129: 
130:     def create_relational_backend(self) -> PostgreSQLBackend | None:
131:         """Create the PostgreSQL relational backend."""
132:         if not self.config.postgresql_url:
133:             logger.warning("PostgreSQL URL not configured, relational backend disabled")
134:             return None
135: 
136:         return PostgreSQLBackend(
137:             self.config.postgresql_url,
138:             echo=self.config.postgresql_echo,
139:             pool_size=self.config.postgresql_pool_size,
140:             max_overflow=self.config.postgresql_max_overflow,
141:         )
142: 
143:     def create_vector_backend(self) -> VectorBackendProtocol | None:
144:         """Create the vector backend based on config."""
145:         vector_config = self.config.vector_config
146: 
147:         # New-style config dispatch
148:         if vector_config is not None:
149:             backend_name = getattr(vector_config, "backend", None)
150:             if backend_name == "pgvector":
151:                 # PgVectorBackend has a specialized constructor
152:                 url = getattr(vector_config, "url", None)
153:                 if not url:
154:                     logger.warning("pgvector URL not configured, vector backend disabled")
155:                     return None
156:                 dim = getattr(vector_config, "embedding_dimension", 1536)
157:                 return PgVectorBackend(
158:                     url,
159:                     embedding_dimension=dim,
160:                     echo=self.config.postgresql_echo,
161:                     pool_size=self.config.postgresql_pool_size,
162:                     max_overflow=self.config.postgresql_max_overflow,
163:                 )
164:             elif backend_name and backend_name in _VECTOR_REGISTRY:
165:                 return self._create_from_registry(_VECTOR_REGISTRY, backend_name, vector_config, "vector")
166: 
167:         # Legacy pgvector path
168:         if not self.config.pgvector_url:
169:             logger.warning("pgvector URL not configured, vector backend disabled")
170:             return None
171: 
172:         return PgVectorBackend(
173:             self.config.pgvector_url,
174:             embedding_dimension=self.config.pgvector_embedding_dimension,
175:             echo=self.config.postgresql_echo,
176:             pool_size=self.config.postgresql_pool_size,
177:             max_overflow=self.config.postgresql_max_overflow,
178:         )
179: 
180:     def create_graph_backend(self) -> GraphBackendProtocol | None:
181:         """Create the graph backend based on config."""
182:         graph_config = self.config.graph_config
183: 
184:         # New-style config dispatch
185:         if graph_config is not None:
186:             backend_name = getattr(graph_config, "backend", None)
187:             if backend_name and backend_name in _GRAPH_REGISTRY:
188:                 return self._create_from_registry(_GRAPH_REGISTRY, backend_name, graph_config, "graph")
189: 
190:         # Legacy Neo4j path
191:         if not self.config.neo4j_url:
192:             logger.warning("Neo4j URL not configured, graph backend disabled")
193:             return None
194: 
195:         try:
196:             from .backends.neo4j import Neo4jBackend
197: 
198:             return Neo4jBackend(
199:                 self.config.neo4j_url,
200:                 user=self.config.neo4j_user,
201:                 password=self.config.neo4j_password,
202:                 database=self.config.neo4j_database,
203:             )
204:         except ImportError:
205:             logger.warning("neo4j package not installed, graph backend disabled")
206:             return None
207: 
208:     def _create_from_registry(
209:         self,
210:         registry: dict[str, tuple[str, str]],
211:         backend_name: str,
212:         config: Any,
213:         role: str,
214:     ) -> Any | None:
215:         """Create a backend instance from registry via lazy import + from_config().
216: 
217:         For ArcadeDB, reuses the same instance when it serves both graph and vector roles.
218:         """
219:         if backend_name == "arcadedb":
220:             # ArcadeDB dual-role: reuse instance for same URL
221:             url = getattr(config, "url", None) or ""
222:             cache_key = f"arcadedb:{url}"
223:             if cache_key in _arcadedb_instances:
224:                 logger.info(f"Reusing ArcadeDB instance for {role} role (url={url})")
225:                 return _arcadedb_instances[cache_key]
226: 
227:         module_path, class_name = registry[backend_name]
228:         cls = _import_backend_class(module_path, class_name)
229:         if cls is None:
230:             logger.warning(f"{backend_name} backend not available (missing dependency), {role} backend disabled")
231:             return None
232: 
233:         if not hasattr(cls, "from_config"):
234:             raise ValueError(f"Backend class {class_name} does not implement from_config()")
235: 
236:         instance = cls.from_config(config)
237: 
238:         if backend_name == "arcadedb":
239:             url = getattr(config, "url", None) or ""
240:             _arcadedb_instances[f"arcadedb:{url}"] = instance
241: 
242:         return instance
243: 
244:     def create_event_store(self) -> EventStoreProtocol | None:
245:         """Create the event store backend."""
246:         if not self.config.event_store_url:
247:             logger.warning("Event store URL not configured, event store disabled")
248:             return None
249: 
250:         # Import here to avoid circular dependency
251:         try:
252:             from .event_store import PostgreSQLEventStore
253: 
254:             return PostgreSQLEventStore(
255:                 self.config.event_store_url,
256:                 echo=self.config.postgresql_echo,
257:                 pool_size=self.config.postgresql_pool_size,
258:                 max_overflow=self.config.postgresql_max_overflow,
259:             )
260:         except ImportError:
261:             logger.warning("Event store not available")
262:             return None
263: 
264:     def create_coordinator(self) -> StorageCoordinator:
265:         """Create a storage coordinator with all configured backends."""
266:         return StorageCoordinator(
267:             relational=self.create_relational_backend(),
268:             vector=self.create_vector_backend(),
269:             graph=self.create_graph_backend(),
270:             event_store=self.create_event_store(),
271:         )
272: 
273: 
274: def create_storage_coordinator(config: dict[str, Any] | StorageConfig | None = None) -> StorageCoordinator:
275:     """Convenience function to create a storage coordinator.
276: 
277:     Args:
278:         config: Configuration dictionary, StorageConfig instance, or None for defaults
279: 
280:     Returns:
281:         Configured StorageCoordinator
282:     """
283:     if config is None:
284:         storage_config = StorageConfig()
285:     elif isinstance(config, StorageConfig):
286:         storage_config = config
287:     else:
288:         storage_config = StorageConfig.from_dict(config)
289: 
290:     factory = StorageFactory(config=storage_config)
291:     return factory.create_coordinator()
````

## File: src/khora/storage/optimize.py
````python
  1: """Storage optimization for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Creates additional indexes on PostgreSQL and Neo4j that improve query
  4: and search performance beyond the base indexes created at schema init time.
  5: Designed to run after bulk data ingestion when tables have enough data
  6: for PostgreSQL's planner statistics to be meaningful.
  7: 
  8: All index creation statements use IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency —
  9: safe to run multiple times.
 10: """
 11: 
 12: from __future__ import annotations
 13: 
 14: from loguru import logger
 15: 
 16: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 17: # PostgreSQL indexes (beyond what SQLAlchemy models already define)
 18: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 19: 
 20: PG_INDEXES = [
 21:     {
 22:         "name": "idx_chunks_namespace_created",
 23:         "sql": ("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_chunks_namespace_created " "ON chunks (namespace_id, created_at DESC)"),
 24:         "purpose": "Temporal filtering within namespace",
 25:     },
 26:     {
 27:         "name": "idx_documents_namespace_created",
 28:         "sql": (
 29:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_documents_namespace_created " "ON documents (namespace_id, created_at DESC)"
 30:         ),
 31:         "purpose": "Document temporal queries",
 32:     },
 33:     {
 34:         "name": "idx_entities_namespace_type_name",
 35:         "sql": (
 36:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_entities_namespace_type_name "
 37:             "ON entities (namespace_id, entity_type, name)"
 38:         ),
 39:         "purpose": "Entity type filtering + name lookup",
 40:     },
 41:     {
 42:         "name": "idx_entities_description_fts",
 43:         "sql": (
 44:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_entities_description_fts "
 45:             "ON entities USING gin (to_tsvector('english', description))"
 46:         ),
 47:         "purpose": "Full-text search on entity descriptions",
 48:     },
 49:     {
 50:         "name": "idx_entities_namespace_confidence",
 51:         "sql": (
 52:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_entities_namespace_confidence "
 53:             "ON entities (namespace_id, confidence DESC)"
 54:         ),
 55:         "purpose": "Confidence-based filtering",
 56:     },
 57:     {
 58:         "name": "idx_relationships_source_target",
 59:         "sql": (
 60:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_relationships_source_target "
 61:             "ON relationships (source_entity_id, target_entity_id)"
 62:         ),
 63:         "purpose": "Relationship traversal",
 64:     },
 65:     {
 66:         "name": "idx_chunks_document_namespace",
 67:         "sql": ("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_chunks_document_namespace " "ON chunks (document_id, namespace_id)"),
 68:         "purpose": "Document-to-chunk lookups",
 69:     },
 70:     # --- Synced from models.py __table_args__ for catch-up on existing databases ---
 71:     {
 72:         "name": "ix_documents_namespace_source_type",
 73:         "sql": (
 74:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ix_documents_namespace_source_type " "ON documents (namespace_id, source_type)"
 75:         ),
 76:         "purpose": "Document queries filtered by source_type within namespace",
 77:     },
 78:     {
 79:         "name": "ix_entities_namespace_mentions",
 80:         "sql": (
 81:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ix_entities_namespace_mentions "
 82:             "ON entities (namespace_id, mention_count DESC)"
 83:         ),
 84:         "purpose": "Entity importance ranking (top N entities in namespace)",
 85:     },
 86:     {
 87:         "name": "ix_relationships_namespace_type",
 88:         "sql": (
 89:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ix_relationships_namespace_type "
 90:             "ON relationships (namespace_id, relationship_type)"
 91:         ),
 92:         "purpose": "Graph-emulated queries filtering by relationship type within namespace",
 93:     },
 94:     {
 95:         "name": "ix_relationships_target_source",
 96:         "sql": (
 97:             "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ix_relationships_target_source "
 98:             "ON relationships (target_entity_id, source_entity_id)"
 99:         ),
100:         "purpose": "Reverse traversal (inbound relationships)",
101:     },
102: ]
103: 
104: #: Tables to run ANALYZE on after index creation.
105: PG_ANALYZE_TABLES = ["chunks", "documents", "entities", "relationships"]
106: 
107: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
108: # Neo4j indexes and constraints
109: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110: 
111: NEO4J_INDEXES = [
112:     {
113:         "name": "entity_namespace_name_type",
114:         "cypher": (
115:             "CREATE INDEX entity_namespace_name_type IF NOT EXISTS "
116:             "FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.namespace_id, e.name, e.entity_type)"
117:         ),
118:         "purpose": "Primary entity lookup (composite)",
119:     },
120:     {
121:         "name": "entity_namespace_id_unique",
122:         "cypher": (
123:             "CREATE CONSTRAINT entity_namespace_id_unique IF NOT EXISTS "
124:             "FOR (e:Entity) REQUIRE (e.namespace_id, e.id) IS UNIQUE"
125:         ),
126:         "purpose": "Prevent duplicate entities",
127:     },
128:     {
129:         "name": "entity_fulltext",
130:         "cypher": (
131:             "CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX entity_fulltext IF NOT EXISTS " "FOR (e:Entity) ON EACH [e.name, e.description]"
132:         ),
133:         "purpose": "Fuzzy name/description search",
134:     },
135:     {
136:         "name": "episode_namespace_occurred",
137:         "cypher": (
138:             "CREATE INDEX episode_namespace_occurred IF NOT EXISTS "
139:             "FOR (e:Episode) ON (e.namespace_id, e.occurred_at)"
140:         ),
141:         "purpose": "Temporal episode queries",
142:     },
143:     {
144:         "name": "relates_to_weight",
145:         "cypher": ("CREATE INDEX relates_to_weight IF NOT EXISTS " "FOR ()-[r:RELATES_TO]-() ON (r.weight)"),
146:         "purpose": "Weighted traversal",
147:     },
148:     {
149:         "name": "mentioned_in_confidence",
150:         "cypher": (
151:             "CREATE INDEX mentioned_in_confidence IF NOT EXISTS " "FOR ()-[r:MENTIONED_IN]-() ON (r.confidence)"
152:         ),
153:         "purpose": "Confidence filtering",
154:     },
155:     # --- Synced from neo4j.py _create_indexes for catch-up on existing databases ---
156:     {
157:         "name": "entity_ns_type",
158:         "cypher": ("CREATE INDEX entity_ns_type IF NOT EXISTS " "FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.namespace_id, e.entity_type)"),
159:         "purpose": "Entity namespace + type composite (list queries filtering by type)",
160:     },
161:     {
162:         "name": "entity_source_tool",
163:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX entity_source_tool IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.source_tool)",
164:         "purpose": "Source-aware queries (e.g. what does Slack say about X?)",
165:     },
166:     {
167:         "name": "entity_confidence",
168:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX entity_confidence IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.confidence)",
169:         "purpose": "Entity confidence threshold filtering",
170:     },
171:     {
172:         "name": "rel_collaborates_ns",
173:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_collaborates_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:COLLABORATES_WITH]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
174:         "purpose": "Namespace filtering on COLLABORATES_WITH",
175:     },
176:     {
177:         "name": "rel_associated_ns",
178:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_associated_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
179:         "purpose": "Namespace filtering on ASSOCIATED_WITH",
180:     },
181:     {
182:         "name": "rel_depends_ns",
183:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_depends_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:DEPENDS_ON]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
184:         "purpose": "Namespace filtering on DEPENDS_ON",
185:     },
186:     {
187:         "name": "rel_owns_ns",
188:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_owns_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:OWNS]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
189:         "purpose": "Namespace filtering on OWNS",
190:     },
191:     {
192:         "name": "rel_works_for_ns",
193:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_works_for_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:WORKS_FOR]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
194:         "purpose": "Namespace filtering on WORKS_FOR",
195:     },
196:     {
197:         "name": "rel_implements_ns",
198:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_implements_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:IMPLEMENTS]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
199:         "purpose": "Namespace filtering on IMPLEMENTS",
200:     },
201:     {
202:         "name": "rel_part_of_ns",
203:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_part_of_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:PART_OF]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
204:         "purpose": "Namespace filtering on PART_OF",
205:     },
206:     {
207:         "name": "rel_collaborates_conf",
208:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_collaborates_conf IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:COLLABORATES_WITH]-() ON (r.confidence)",
209:         "purpose": "Confidence filtering on COLLABORATES_WITH",
210:     },
211:     {
212:         "name": "rel_associated_conf",
213:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_associated_conf IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-() ON (r.confidence)",
214:         "purpose": "Confidence filtering on ASSOCIATED_WITH",
215:     },
216:     {
217:         "name": "rel_depends_conf",
218:         "cypher": "CREATE INDEX rel_depends_conf IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:DEPENDS_ON]-() ON (r.confidence)",
219:         "purpose": "Confidence filtering on DEPENDS_ON",
220:     },
221: ]
222: 
223: 
224: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
225: # Public API
226: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
227: 
228: 
229: async def optimize_postgresql(engine) -> dict:
230:     """Create optimal PostgreSQL indexes and run ANALYZE.
231: 
232:     Executes raw DDL against the provided SQLAlchemy async engine,
233:     so that callers don't need to provide a raw connection URL.
234: 
235:     Args:
236:         engine: An ``sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio.AsyncEngine`` instance.
237: 
238:     Returns:
239:         Dict with ``indexes_created``, ``tables_analyzed``, and ``errors``.
240:     """
241:     from sqlalchemy import text
242: 
243:     result = {
244:         "indexes_created": 0,
245:         "tables_analyzed": 0,
246:         "errors": [],
247:     }
248: 
249:     async with engine.begin() as conn:
250:         for idx in PG_INDEXES:
251:             try:
252:                 logger.debug(f"Creating index {idx['name']} ({idx['purpose']})")
253:                 await conn.execute(text(idx["sql"]))
254:                 result["indexes_created"] += 1
255:             except Exception as e:
256:                 msg = f"Index {idx['name']}: {e}"
257:                 result["errors"].append(msg)
258:                 logger.warning(msg)
259: 
260:         for table in PG_ANALYZE_TABLES:
261:             try:
262:                 logger.debug(f"Analyzing table {table}")
263:                 await conn.execute(text(f"ANALYZE {table}"))
264:                 result["tables_analyzed"] += 1
265:             except Exception as e:
266:                 msg = f"ANALYZE {table}: {e}"
267:                 result["errors"].append(msg)
268:                 logger.warning(msg)
269: 
270:     return result
271: 
272: 
273: async def optimize_neo4j(driver, *, database: str = "neo4j") -> dict:
274:     """Create optimal Neo4j indexes and constraints.
275: 
276:     Args:
277:         driver: An ``neo4j.AsyncDriver`` instance.
278:         database: Neo4j database name.
279: 
280:     Returns:
281:         Dict with ``indexes_created`` and ``errors``.
282:     """
283:     result = {
284:         "indexes_created": 0,
285:         "errors": [],
286:     }
287: 
288:     async with driver.session(database=database) as session:
289:         for idx in NEO4J_INDEXES:
290:             try:
291:                 logger.debug(f"Creating Neo4j index {idx['name']} ({idx['purpose']})")
292:                 await session.run(idx["cypher"])
293:                 result["indexes_created"] += 1
294:             except Exception as e:
295:                 msg = f"Neo4j index {idx['name']}: {e}"
296:                 result["errors"].append(msg)
297:                 logger.warning(msg)
298: 
299:     return result
300: 
301: 
302: async def optimize_storage(coordinator) -> dict:
303:     """Run all optimizations against a connected StorageCoordinator.
304: 
305:     This is the main entry point for callers that already have a
306:     ``StorageCoordinator`` (e.g. via ``MemoryLake.storage``).
307: 
308:     Args:
309:         coordinator: A connected ``StorageCoordinator`` instance.
310: 
311:     Returns:
312:         Combined results: ``{"postgresql": {...}, "neo4j": {...}}``.
313:     """
314:     results: dict[str, dict | None] = {"postgresql": None, "neo4j": None}
315: 
316:     # Optimize PostgreSQL / pgvector (they share the same engine)
317:     backend = coordinator.vector or coordinator.relational
318:     if backend is not None:
319:         engine = getattr(backend, "_engine", None)
320:         if engine is not None:
321:             results["postgresql"] = await optimize_postgresql(engine)
322:         else:
323:             logger.warning("No SQLAlchemy engine found on backend; skipping PostgreSQL optimization")
324: 
325:     # Optimize Neo4j
326:     graph = coordinator.graph
327:     if graph is not None:
328:         driver = getattr(graph, "_driver", None)
329:         database = getattr(graph, "_database", "neo4j")
330:         if driver is not None:
331:             results["neo4j"] = await optimize_neo4j(driver, database=database)
332:         else:
333:             logger.warning("No Neo4j driver found on backend; skipping Neo4j optimization")
334: 
335:     return results
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/__init__.py
````python
 1: """Khora internal telemetry.
 2: 
 3: Records LLM usage, storage operations, and pipeline performance to a
 4: dedicated PostgreSQL database.  Enabled when ``KHORA_TELEMETRY_DATABASE_URL``
 5: is set; otherwise a zero-cost :class:`NoOpCollector` is used.
 6: 
 7: Quick start::
 8: 
 9:     from khora.telemetry import get_collector
10: 
11:     get_collector().record_llm_call(
12:         operation="entity_extraction",
13:         model="gpt-4o-mini",
14:         prompt_tokens=120,
15:         completion_tokens=350,
16:         total_tokens=470,
17:         latency_ms=812.3,
18:     )
19: """
20: 
21: from __future__ import annotations
22: 
23: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
24: 
25: from .collector import TelemetryCollector
26: from .context import clear_trace_id, ensure_trace_id, get_trace_id, set_trace_id
27: from .noop import NoOpCollector
28: 
29: if TYPE_CHECKING:
30:     from .config import TelemetryConfig
31: 
32: __all__ = [
33:     "TelemetryCollector",
34:     "NoOpCollector",
35:     "get_collector",
36:     "init_telemetry",
37:     "shutdown_telemetry",
38:     "get_trace_id",
39:     "set_trace_id",
40:     "ensure_trace_id",
41:     "clear_trace_id",
42: ]
43: 
44: _collector: TelemetryCollector | NoOpCollector = NoOpCollector()
45: 
46: 
47: def get_collector() -> TelemetryCollector | NoOpCollector:
48:     """Return the current global telemetry collector."""
49:     return _collector
50: 
51: 
52: async def init_telemetry(config: TelemetryConfig | None = None) -> TelemetryCollector | NoOpCollector:
53:     """Initialise the telemetry subsystem.
54: 
55:     If *config* is ``None`` the configuration is read from environment
56:     variables.  When no ``KHORA_TELEMETRY_DATABASE_URL`` is found a
57:     :class:`NoOpCollector` is returned.
58:     """
59:     global _collector
60: 
61:     if config is None:
62:         from .config import TelemetryConfig
63: 
64:         config = TelemetryConfig.from_env()
65: 
66:     if not config.database_url:
67:         _collector = NoOpCollector()
68:         return _collector
69: 
70:     from .session import create_telemetry_engine
71: 
72:     engine = create_telemetry_engine(config.database_url)
73:     _collector = TelemetryCollector(
74:         engine,
75:         service_name=config.service_name,
76:         flush_interval=config.flush_interval_seconds,
77:         flush_threshold=config.flush_threshold,
78:     )
79:     await _collector.start()
80:     return _collector
81: 
82: 
83: async def shutdown_telemetry() -> None:
84:     """Shut down the telemetry collector (final flush + engine dispose)."""
85:     global _collector
86:     await _collector.shutdown()
87:     _collector = NoOpCollector()
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/instrument.py
````python
  1: """Decorators and context managers for telemetry instrumentation."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: import functools
  6: import time
  7: from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
  8: from typing import Any
  9: from uuid import UUID
 10: 
 11: 
 12: def instrument_llm(operation: str):
 13:     """Decorator for async functions that make LLM calls.
 14: 
 15:     Extracts model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, and total_tokens
 16:     from LiteLLM ``ModelResponse.usage``.  Automatically populates
 17:     trace_id and parent_event_id from context vars.
 18: 
 19:     Args:
 20:         operation: Logical operation name (e.g. "entity_extraction", "embedding").
 21:     """
 22: 
 23:     def decorator(fn):
 24:         @functools.wraps(fn)
 25:         async def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
 26:             from . import get_collector
 27: 
 28:             collector = get_collector()
 29:             start = time.perf_counter()
 30:             status = "success"
 31:             error_msg: str | None = None
 32:             result = None
 33:             try:
 34:                 result = await fn(*args, **kwargs)
 35:                 return result
 36:             except Exception as exc:
 37:                 status = "error"
 38:                 error_msg = str(exc)[:500]
 39:                 raise
 40:             finally:
 41:                 latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
 42:                 # Extract model and token counts from LiteLLM response
 43:                 model = ""
 44:                 prompt_tokens = 0
 45:                 completion_tokens = 0
 46:                 total_tokens = 0
 47:                 if result is not None:
 48:                     usage = getattr(result, "usage", None)
 49:                     if usage is not None:
 50:                         prompt_tokens = getattr(usage, "prompt_tokens", 0) or 0
 51:                         completion_tokens = getattr(usage, "completion_tokens", 0) or 0
 52:                         total_tokens = getattr(usage, "total_tokens", 0) or 0
 53:                     model = getattr(result, "model", "") or ""
 54:                 collector.record_llm_call(
 55:                     operation=operation,
 56:                     model=model,
 57:                     prompt_tokens=prompt_tokens,
 58:                     completion_tokens=completion_tokens,
 59:                     total_tokens=total_tokens,
 60:                     latency_ms=latency_ms,
 61:                     status=status,
 62:                     error_message=error_msg,
 63:                 )
 64: 
 65:         return wrapper
 66: 
 67:     return decorator
 68: 
 69: 
 70: def instrument_storage(backend: str, operation: str):
 71:     """Decorator for async storage methods.
 72: 
 73:     Extracts namespace_id from kwargs or first UUID positional arg.
 74:     Automatically populates trace_id and parent_event_id from context vars.
 75: 
 76:     Args:
 77:         backend: Storage backend name (e.g. "postgresql", "pgvector", "neo4j").
 78:         operation: Operation name (e.g. "create_document").
 79:     """
 80: 
 81:     def decorator(fn):
 82:         @functools.wraps(fn)
 83:         async def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
 84:             from . import get_collector
 85: 
 86:             collector = get_collector()
 87:             start = time.perf_counter()
 88:             status = "success"
 89:             error_msg: str | None = None
 90:             record_count = 0
 91:             namespace_id: UUID | None = kwargs.get("namespace_id")
 92: 
 93:             # Try to extract namespace_id from positional args if not in kwargs
 94:             if namespace_id is None:
 95:                 for arg in args:
 96:                     if isinstance(arg, UUID):
 97:                         namespace_id = arg
 98:                         break
 99: 
100:             try:
101:                 result = await fn(*args, **kwargs)
102:                 # Try to guess record count from result
103:                 if isinstance(result, list):
104:                     record_count = len(result)
105:                 elif result is not None:
106:                     record_count = 1
107:                 return result
108:             except Exception as exc:
109:                 status = "error"
110:                 error_msg = str(exc)[:500]
111:                 raise
112:             finally:
113:                 latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
114:                 collector.record_storage_op(
115:                     backend=backend,
116:                     operation=operation,
117:                     latency_ms=latency_ms,
118:                     record_count=record_count,
119:                     status=status,
120:                     error_message=error_msg,
121:                     namespace_id=namespace_id,
122:                 )
123: 
124:         return wrapper
125: 
126:     return decorator
127: 
128: 
129: @asynccontextmanager
130: async def pipeline_stage(
131:     pipeline_name: str,
132:     stage: str,
133:     run_id: UUID | None = None,
134:     *,
135:     namespace_id: UUID | None = None,
136:     input_count: int = 0,
137:     extra_metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
138: ):
139:     """Async context manager that records a pipeline stage to telemetry.
140: 
141:     Sets parent_event_id in context so that child LLM/storage calls
142:     auto-link to this pipeline stage.
143: 
144:     Usage::
145: 
146:         async with pipeline_stage("ingestion", "chunking", run_id, input_count=1) as ctx:
147:             chunks = await chunk_document(doc)
148:             ctx["output_count"] = len(chunks)
149: 
150:     Args:
151:         pipeline_name: Pipeline identifier (e.g. "ingestion", "query").
152:         stage: Stage name (e.g. "chunking", "embedding").
153:         run_id: UUID grouping stages in one pipeline execution.
154:         namespace_id: Optional namespace ID for the event.
155:         input_count: Number of items going into this stage.
156:         extra_metadata: Optional extra metadata dict.
157:     """
158:     from . import get_collector
159:     from .context import get_parent_event_id, set_parent_event_id
160: 
161:     collector = get_collector()
162:     # Save previous parent so we can restore it
163:     prev_parent = get_parent_event_id()
164:     # Use a simple incrementing ID for parent linking (based on buffer position)
165:     # Since we don't have the DB-assigned id yet, use the run_id hash as a stable reference
166:     stage_id = hash((pipeline_name, stage, run_id)) & 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF  # positive int64
167:     set_parent_event_id(stage_id)
168: 
169:     ctx: dict[str, Any] = {"output_count": 0}
170:     start = time.perf_counter()
171:     status = "success"
172:     error_msg: str | None = None
173:     try:
174:         yield ctx
175:     except Exception as exc:
176:         status = "error"
177:         error_msg = str(exc)[:500]
178:         raise
179:     finally:
180:         set_parent_event_id(prev_parent)
181:         latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
182:         collector.record_pipeline_stage(
183:             pipeline=pipeline_name,
184:             stage=stage,
185:             run_id=run_id,
186:             latency_ms=latency_ms,
187:             input_count=input_count,
188:             output_count=ctx.get("output_count", 0),
189:             status=status,
190:             error_message=error_msg,
191:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
192:             metadata=extra_metadata or {},
193:         )
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/models.py
````python
 1: """Pydantic models for telemetry events."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from datetime import UTC, datetime
 6: from typing import Any
 7: from uuid import UUID
 8: 
 9: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
10: 
11: 
12: class LLMEvent(BaseModel):
13:     """A single LLM API call event."""
14: 
15:     timestamp: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(UTC))
16:     service_name: str = "khora"
17:     operation: str = ""
18:     model: str = ""
19:     prompt_tokens: int = 0
20:     completion_tokens: int = 0
21:     total_tokens: int = 0
22:     latency_ms: float = 0.0
23:     status: str = "success"
24:     error_message: str | None = None
25:     namespace_id: UUID | None = None
26:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
27:     # Tracing
28:     trace_id: UUID | None = None
29:     parent_event_id: int | None = None
30:     cache_hit: bool = False
31:     batch_size: int = 1
32: 
33: 
34: class StorageEvent(BaseModel):
35:     """A single storage operation event."""
36: 
37:     timestamp: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(UTC))
38:     service_name: str = "khora"
39:     backend: str = ""
40:     operation: str = ""
41:     latency_ms: float = 0.0
42:     record_count: int = 0
43:     status: str = "success"
44:     error_message: str | None = None
45:     namespace_id: UUID | None = None
46:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
47:     # Tracing
48:     trace_id: UUID | None = None
49:     parent_event_id: int | None = None
50: 
51: 
52: class PipelineEvent(BaseModel):
53:     """A single pipeline stage event."""
54: 
55:     timestamp: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(UTC))
56:     service_name: str = "khora"
57:     pipeline: str = ""
58:     stage: str = ""
59:     run_id: UUID | None = None
60:     latency_ms: float = 0.0
61:     input_count: int = 0
62:     output_count: int = 0
63:     status: str = "success"
64:     error_message: str | None = None
65:     namespace_id: UUID | None = None
66:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
67:     # Tracing
68:     trace_id: UUID | None = None
69:     parent_event_id: int | None = None
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/tables.py
````python
 1: """SQLAlchemy table definitions for telemetry events.
 2: 
 3: Uses Core tables (not ORM) for lightweight batch inserts.
 4: Tables are auto-created via ``metadata.create_all`` -- no Alembic needed.
 5: 
 6: Schema version is tracked via SCHEMA_VERSION; the collector drops and
 7: recreates tables when the version changes.
 8: """
 9: 
10: from __future__ import annotations
11: 
12: import sqlalchemy as sa
13: 
14: SCHEMA_VERSION = 2
15: 
16: metadata = sa.MetaData()
17: 
18: llm_events = sa.Table(
19:     "llm_events",
20:     metadata,
21:     sa.Column("id", sa.BigInteger, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True),
22:     sa.Column("timestamp", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False),
23:     sa.Column("service_name", sa.String(64), nullable=False),
24:     sa.Column("operation", sa.String(64), nullable=False, default=""),
25:     sa.Column("model", sa.String(128), nullable=False, default=""),
26:     sa.Column("prompt_tokens", sa.Integer, nullable=False, default=0),
27:     sa.Column("completion_tokens", sa.Integer, nullable=False, default=0),
28:     sa.Column("total_tokens", sa.Integer, nullable=False, default=0),
29:     sa.Column("latency_ms", sa.Float, nullable=False, default=0.0),
30:     sa.Column("status", sa.String(16), nullable=False, default="success"),
31:     sa.Column("error_message", sa.Text, nullable=True),
32:     sa.Column("namespace_id", sa.Uuid, nullable=True),
33:     sa.Column("metadata", sa.JSON, nullable=True),
34:     # Tracing columns (v2)
35:     sa.Column("trace_id", sa.Uuid, nullable=True, index=True),
36:     sa.Column("parent_event_id", sa.BigInteger, nullable=True),
37:     sa.Column("cache_hit", sa.Boolean, nullable=False, server_default=sa.text("false")),
38:     sa.Column("batch_size", sa.Integer, nullable=False, server_default=sa.text("1")),
39: )
40: 
41: storage_events = sa.Table(
42:     "storage_events",
43:     metadata,
44:     sa.Column("id", sa.BigInteger, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True),
45:     sa.Column("timestamp", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False),
46:     sa.Column("service_name", sa.String(64), nullable=False),
47:     sa.Column("backend", sa.String(32), nullable=False, default=""),
48:     sa.Column("operation", sa.String(64), nullable=False, default=""),
49:     sa.Column("latency_ms", sa.Float, nullable=False, default=0.0),
50:     sa.Column("record_count", sa.Integer, nullable=False, default=0),
51:     sa.Column("status", sa.String(16), nullable=False, default="success"),
52:     sa.Column("error_message", sa.Text, nullable=True),
53:     sa.Column("namespace_id", sa.Uuid, nullable=True),
54:     sa.Column("metadata", sa.JSON, nullable=True),
55:     # Tracing columns (v2)
56:     sa.Column("trace_id", sa.Uuid, nullable=True, index=True),
57:     sa.Column("parent_event_id", sa.BigInteger, nullable=True),
58: )
59: 
60: pipeline_events = sa.Table(
61:     "pipeline_events",
62:     metadata,
63:     sa.Column("id", sa.BigInteger, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True),
64:     sa.Column("timestamp", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False),
65:     sa.Column("service_name", sa.String(64), nullable=False),
66:     sa.Column("pipeline", sa.String(64), nullable=False, default=""),
67:     sa.Column("stage", sa.String(64), nullable=False, default=""),
68:     sa.Column("run_id", sa.Uuid, nullable=True),
69:     sa.Column("latency_ms", sa.Float, nullable=False, default=0.0),
70:     sa.Column("input_count", sa.Integer, nullable=False, default=0),
71:     sa.Column("output_count", sa.Integer, nullable=False, default=0),
72:     sa.Column("status", sa.String(16), nullable=False, default="success"),
73:     sa.Column("error_message", sa.Text, nullable=True),
74:     sa.Column("namespace_id", sa.Uuid, nullable=True),
75:     sa.Column("metadata", sa.JSON, nullable=True),
76:     # Tracing columns (v2)
77:     sa.Column("trace_id", sa.Uuid, nullable=True, index=True),
78:     sa.Column("parent_event_id", sa.BigInteger, nullable=True),
79: )
````

## File: src/khora/_accel.py
````python
  1: """Accelerated operations with graceful fallbacks.
  2: 
  3: Provides optimized implementations of CPU-intensive operations using
  4: numpy (cosine similarity) and rapidfuzz (string similarity). Falls back
  5: to pure-Python implementations when those libraries are not available.
  6: """
  7: 
  8: from __future__ import annotations
  9: 
 10: import math
 11: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
 12: 
 13: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 14:     pass
 15: 
 16: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 17: # Optional dependency detection
 18: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 19: 
 20: try:
 21:     import numpy as np
 22: 
 23:     _HAS_NUMPY = True
 24: except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
 25:     _HAS_NUMPY = False
 26: 
 27: try:
 28:     from rapidfuzz.distance import Levenshtein as _rf_lev
 29:     from rapidfuzz.fuzz import ratio as _rf_ratio
 30: 
 31:     _HAS_RAPIDFUZZ = True
 32: except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
 33:     _HAS_RAPIDFUZZ = False
 34: 
 35: 
 36: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 37: # Cosine similarity
 38: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 39: 
 40: 
 41: def cosine_similarity(vec1: list[float], vec2: list[float]) -> float:
 42:     """Compute cosine similarity between two vectors.
 43: 
 44:     Uses numpy when available (~50-200x faster for 1536-dim vectors).
 45:     """
 46:     if len(vec1) != len(vec2):
 47:         return 0.0
 48: 
 49:     if _HAS_NUMPY:
 50:         a = np.asarray(vec1, dtype=np.float32)
 51:         b = np.asarray(vec2, dtype=np.float32)
 52:         dot = float(np.dot(a, b))
 53:         na = float(np.linalg.norm(a))
 54:         nb = float(np.linalg.norm(b))
 55:         if na == 0.0 or nb == 0.0:
 56:             return 0.0
 57:         return dot / (na * nb)
 58: 
 59:     # Pure-Python fallback
 60:     dot = 0.0
 61:     norm1 = 0.0
 62:     norm2 = 0.0
 63:     for a, b in zip(vec1, vec2):
 64:         dot += a * b
 65:         norm1 += a * a
 66:         norm2 += b * b
 67: 
 68:     if norm1 == 0.0 or norm2 == 0.0:
 69:         return 0.0
 70:     return dot / (math.sqrt(norm1) * math.sqrt(norm2))
 71: 
 72: 
 73: def batch_cosine_similarity(
 74:     query: list[float],
 75:     candidates: list[list[float]],
 76:     threshold: float = 0.0,
 77: ) -> list[tuple[int, float]]:
 78:     """Compute cosine similarity between a query vector and a matrix of candidates.
 79: 
 80:     Returns (index, similarity) pairs above threshold, sorted descending.
 81:     Uses numpy batch matmul when available.
 82:     """
 83:     if not candidates:
 84:         return []
 85: 
 86:     if _HAS_NUMPY:
 87:         q = np.asarray(query, dtype=np.float32)
 88:         mat = np.asarray(candidates, dtype=np.float32)
 89: 
 90:         q_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(q))
 91:         if q_norm == 0.0:
 92:             return []
 93: 
 94:         norms = np.linalg.norm(mat, axis=1)
 95:         # Avoid division by zero
 96:         safe_norms = np.where(norms == 0.0, 1.0, norms)
 97:         sims = (mat @ q) / (safe_norms * q_norm)
 98:         # Zero out entries where candidate norm was zero
 99:         sims = np.where(norms == 0.0, 0.0, sims)
100: 
101:         results = []
102:         for i in range(len(sims)):
103:             s = float(sims[i])
104:             if s >= threshold:
105:                 results.append((i, s))
106:         results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
107:         return results
108: 
109:     # Pure-Python fallback
110:     results = []
111:     for i, cand in enumerate(candidates):
112:         s = cosine_similarity(query, cand)
113:         if s >= threshold:
114:             results.append((i, s))
115:     results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
116:     return results
117: 
118: 
119: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
120: # Levenshtein similarity
121: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122: 
123: 
124: def levenshtein_similarity(s1: str, s2: str) -> float:
125:     """Normalized Levenshtein similarity (1.0 = identical).
126: 
127:     Uses rapidfuzz when available (~5-10x faster).
128:     """
129:     a, b = s1.lower(), s2.lower()
130:     if a == b:
131:         return 1.0
132:     if not a or not b:
133:         return 0.0
134: 
135:     if _HAS_RAPIDFUZZ:
136:         return _rf_lev.normalized_similarity(a, b)
137: 
138:     # Pure-Python single-row DP fallback
139:     la, lb = len(a), len(b)
140:     prev = list(range(lb + 1))
141:     for i in range(1, la + 1):
142:         curr = [i] + [0] * lb
143:         for j in range(1, lb + 1):
144:             cost = 0 if a[i - 1] == b[j - 1] else 1
145:             curr[j] = min(prev[j] + 1, curr[j - 1] + 1, prev[j - 1] + cost)
146:         prev = curr
147: 
148:     distance = prev[lb]
149:     return 1.0 - (distance / max(la, lb))
150: 
151: 
152: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
153: # Sequence matching (SequenceMatcher replacement)
154: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
155: 
156: 
157: def sequence_match_ratio(s1: str, s2: str) -> float:
158:     """Compute sequence match ratio between two strings.
159: 
160:     Uses rapidfuzz.fuzz.ratio when available (~3-10x faster than
161:     difflib.SequenceMatcher). Returns a float in [0.0, 1.0].
162:     """
163:     if _HAS_RAPIDFUZZ:
164:         return _rf_ratio(s1, s2) / 100.0
165: 
166:     from difflib import SequenceMatcher
167: 
168:     return SequenceMatcher(None, s1, s2).ratio()
````

## File: tests/unit/engines/test_skeleton.py
````python
  1: """Unit tests for skeleton-based indexing."""
  2: 
  3: from uuid import uuid4
  4: 
  5: import pytest
  6: 
  7: from khora.engines.skeleton.backends import TemporalChunk
  8: from khora.engines.skeleton.skeleton import ChunkNode, KeywordNode, SkeletonIndexer
  9: 
 10: 
 11: class TestChunkNode:
 12:     """Tests for ChunkNode dataclass."""
 13: 
 14:     def test_create_chunk_node(self):
 15:         """Test creating a chunk node."""
 16:         chunk_id = uuid4()
 17:         keywords = {"python", "testing", "code"}
 18: 
 19:         node = ChunkNode(
 20:             chunk_id=chunk_id,
 21:             content="Python testing code example",
 22:             keywords=keywords,
 23:             pagerank_score=0.5,
 24:             is_core=True,
 25:         )
 26: 
 27:         assert node.chunk_id == chunk_id
 28:         assert node.content == "Python testing code example"
 29:         assert node.keywords == keywords
 30:         assert node.pagerank_score == 0.5
 31:         assert node.is_core is True
 32: 
 33:     def test_chunk_node_defaults(self):
 34:         """Test chunk node default values."""
 35:         node = ChunkNode(
 36:             chunk_id=uuid4(),
 37:             content="test",
 38:         )
 39: 
 40:         assert node.keywords == set()
 41:         assert node.pagerank_score == 0.0
 42:         assert node.is_core is False
 43: 
 44:

 46:     """Tests for KeywordNode dataclass."""

 48:     def test_create_keyword_node(self):
 49:         """Test creating a keyword node."""
 50:         chunk_ids = {uuid4(), uuid4()}
 51: 
 52:         node = KeywordNode(
 53:             keyword="python",
 54:             chunk_ids=chunk_ids,
 55:             idf_score=2.5,
 56:         )
 57: 
 58:         assert node.keyword == "python"
 59:         assert node.chunk_ids == chunk_ids
 60:         assert node.idf_score == 2.5
 61: 
 62:     def test_keyword_node_defaults(self):
 63:         """Test keyword node default values."""
 64:         node = KeywordNode(keyword="test")
 65: 
 66:         assert node.chunk_ids == set()
 67:         assert node.idf_score == 0.0

 69: 
 70: class TestSkeletonIndexer:
 71:     """Tests for SkeletonIndexer."""
 72: 
 73:     @pytest.fixture
 74:     def indexer(self):
 75:         """Create a skeleton indexer."""
 76:         return SkeletonIndexer(core_ratio=0.3)
 77: 
 78:     def test_extract_keywords(self, indexer):
 79:         """Test keyword extraction from content."""
 80:         content = "Python is a great programming language for data science and machine learning."
 81:         keywords = indexer._extract_keywords(content)
 82: 
 83:         # Should include meaningful words
 84:         assert "python" in keywords
 85:         assert "programming" in keywords
 86:         assert "language" in keywords
 87:         assert "data" in keywords
 88:         assert "science" in keywords
 89:         assert "machine" in keywords
 90:         assert "learning" in keywords
 91: 
 92:         # Should NOT include stopwords
 93:         assert "is" not in keywords
 94:         assert "a" not in keywords
 95:         assert "the" not in keywords
 96:         assert "for" not in keywords
 97:         assert "and" not in keywords
 98: 
 99:     def test_extract_keywords_short_words_filtered(self, indexer):
100:         """Test that short words are filtered out."""
101:         content = "I am at a go to do it"
102:         keywords = indexer._extract_keywords(content)
103: 
104:         # All words are too short (< 3 chars) or stopwords
105:         assert len(keywords) == 0

107:     def test_add_chunk(self, indexer):
108:         """Test adding a chunk to the indexer."""

116:         indexer.add_chunk(chunk)
117: 
118:         assert chunk.id in indexer._chunks
119:         assert "python" in indexer._keywords
120:         assert "programming" in indexer._keywords
121:         assert "asyncio" in indexer._keywords
122: 
123:         # Check bidirectional links

148: 
149:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)

155:     def test_build_skeleton_selects_core(self, indexer):
156:         """Test that build_skeleton selects core chunks."""
157:         # Create interconnected chunks
158:         chunks = [

161:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
162:                 document_id=uuid4(),
163:                 content="Python programming with asyncio and FastAPI web framework",
164:             ),
165:             TemporalChunk(
166:                 id=uuid4(),
167:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
168:                 document_id=uuid4(),
169:                 content="Python asyncio tutorial for beginners",
170:             ),
171:             TemporalChunk(
172:                 id=uuid4(),
173:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
174:                 document_id=uuid4(),
175:                 content="FastAPI framework documentation guide",
176:             ),
177:             TemporalChunk(
178:                 id=uuid4(),
179:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
180:                 document_id=uuid4(),
181:                 content="Isolated topic about gardening plants",
182:             ),
183:         ]
184: 
185:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
186:         core_ids = indexer.build_skeleton()
187: 
188:         # With 30% core_ratio and 4 chunks, should have ~1-2 core
189:         assert len(core_ids) >= 1
190:         assert len(core_ids) <= 2
191: 
192:         # Highly connected chunks (Python, asyncio, FastAPI) should be core
193:         # Isolated chunk (gardening) should NOT be core

195:         assert gardening_chunk.id not in core_ids
196: 
197:     def test_get_chunks_by_keyword(self, indexer):

201:

216:

227:     def test_get_related_chunks(self, indexer):
228:         """Test finding related chunks via keyword overlap."""
229:         chunk1_id = uuid4()
230:         chunk2_id = uuid4()
231:         chunk3_id = uuid4()
232: 
233:         chunks = [
234:             TemporalChunk(

236:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
237:                 document_id=uuid4(),
238:                 content="Python asyncio concurrent programming",
239:             ),
240:             TemporalChunk(
241:                 id=chunk2_id,
242:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
243:                 document_id=uuid4(),
244:                 content="Python asyncio tutorial examples",
245:             ),
246:             TemporalChunk(
247:                 id=chunk3_id,
248:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
249:                 document_id=uuid4(),
250:                 content="JavaScript React components",
251:             ),
252:         ]
253: 
254:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
255:         indexer.build_skeleton()
256: 
257:         # Find chunks related to chunk1
258:         related = indexer.get_related_chunks(chunk1_id, limit=5)
259: 
260:         # chunk2 should be most related (shares python, asyncio)
261:         assert len(related) > 0
262:         related_ids = [r[0] for r in related]
263:         assert chunk2_id in related_ids
264: 
265:         # chunk3 has no overlap, should not be in results
266:         if len(related) <= 2:
267:             assert (
268:                 chunk3_id not in related_ids
269:                 or related[[r[0] for r in related].index(chunk3_id) if chunk3_id in related_ids else -1][1] < 0.5
270:             )
271: 
272:     def test_search_by_keywords(self, indexer):
273:         """Test searching by multiple keywords."""

275:             TemporalChunk(
276:                 id=uuid4(),

278:                 document_id=uuid4(),
279:                 content="Python asyncio programming",
280:             ),
281:             TemporalChunk(
282:                 id=uuid4(),
283:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
284:                 document_id=uuid4(),
285:                 content="Python web framework",

292:             ),

308:     def test_is_core_chunk(self, indexer):
309:         """Test checking if a chunk is core."""
310:         chunks = [
311:             TemporalChunk(
312:                 id=uuid4(),
313:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
314:                 document_id=uuid4(),
315:                 content="Python asyncio concurrent programming FastAPI web framework database",
316:             ),
317:             TemporalChunk(
318:                 id=uuid4(),
319:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
320:                 document_id=uuid4(),
321:                 content="Isolated unrelated content about nothing",
322:             ),
323:         ]
324: 
325:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
326:         core_ids = indexer.build_skeleton()
327: 
328:         # Core chunks should return True
329:         for cid in core_ids:
330:             assert indexer.is_core_chunk(cid) is True
331: 
332:         # Unknown chunk should return False
333:         assert indexer.is_core_chunk(uuid4()) is False
334: 
335:     def test_get_pagerank_score(self, indexer):
336:         """Test getting PageRank score."""
337:         chunks = [
338:             TemporalChunk(
339:                 id=uuid4(),
340:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
341:                 document_id=uuid4(),
342:                 content="Central hub connecting everything python asyncio fastapi",
343:             ),
344:             TemporalChunk(
345:                 id=uuid4(),
346:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
347:                 document_id=uuid4(),
348:                 content="Python asyncio only",
349:             ),
350:         ]
351: 
352:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
353:         indexer.build_skeleton()
354: 
355:         # All chunks should have a PageRank score > 0
356:         for chunk in chunks:
357:             score = indexer.get_pagerank_score(chunk.id)
358:             assert score > 0
359: 
360:         # Unknown chunk should return 0
361:         assert indexer.get_pagerank_score(uuid4()) == 0.0
362: 
363: 
364: class TestPageRankCalculation:
365:     """Tests for PageRank calculation."""
366: 
367:     def test_pagerank_convergence(self):
368:         """Test that PageRank converges."""
369:         indexer = SkeletonIndexer(core_ratio=0.5, max_iterations=50)
370: 
371:         # Create a simple chain of connected chunks
372:         chunks = [
373:             TemporalChunk(
374:                 id=uuid4(),
375:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
376:                 document_id=uuid4(),
377:                 content="Python programming basics introduction",
378:             ),
379:             TemporalChunk(
380:                 id=uuid4(),
381:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
382:                 document_id=uuid4(),
383:                 content="Python programming advanced topics",
384:             ),
385:             TemporalChunk(
386:                 id=uuid4(),
387:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
388:                 document_id=uuid4(),
389:                 content="Python advanced machine learning",
390:             ),
391:         ]
392: 
393:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
394:         indexer.build_skeleton()
395: 
396:         # All chunks should have positive PageRank
397:         for chunk in chunks:
398:             assert indexer.get_pagerank_score(chunk.id) > 0
399: 
400:     def test_empty_indexer(self):
401:         """Test behavior with empty indexer."""
402:         indexer = SkeletonIndexer()
403: 
404:         core_ids = indexer.build_skeleton()
405:         assert core_ids == []
406: 
407:         assert indexer.get_core_chunks() == []
408:         assert indexer.get_chunks_by_keyword("test") == []
409:         assert indexer.get_related_chunks(uuid4()) == []
410:         assert indexer.search_by_keywords(["test"]) == []
411: 
412:     def test_single_chunk(self):
413:         """Test with single chunk."""
414:         indexer = SkeletonIndexer(core_ratio=1.0)
415: 
416:         chunk = TemporalChunk(
417:             id=uuid4(),
418:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
419:             document_id=uuid4(),
420:             content="Standalone content",
421:         )
422: 
423:         indexer.add_chunk(chunk)
424:         core_ids = indexer.build_skeleton()
425: 
426:         # Single chunk should be core
427:         assert chunk.id in core_ids
````
