````python
429: 
430: class TestSkeletonCostOptimization:
431:     """Tests verifying cost optimization properties."""
432: 
433:     def test_core_ratio_respected(self):
434:         """Test that core ratio is approximately respected."""
435:         indexer = SkeletonIndexer(core_ratio=0.2)  # 20%
436: 
437:         # Create 20 chunks
438:         chunks = [
439:             TemporalChunk(
440:                 id=uuid4(),
441:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
442:                 document_id=uuid4(),
443:                 content=f"Content about topic {i} with keywords",
444:             )
445:             for i in range(20)
446:         ]
447: 
448:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
449:         core_ids = indexer.build_skeleton()
450: 
451:         # Should have approximately 20% = 4 core chunks (±1 for rounding)
452:         assert 3 <= len(core_ids) <= 5
453: 
454:     def test_highly_connected_chunks_become_core(self):
455:         """Test that highly connected chunks become core."""
456:         indexer = SkeletonIndexer(core_ratio=0.3)
457: 
458:         # Create a hub chunk that shares keywords with many others
459:         hub_id = uuid4()
460:         chunks = [
461:             TemporalChunk(
462:                 id=hub_id,
463:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
464:                 document_id=uuid4(),
465:                 content="Python asyncio FastAPI React JavaScript TypeScript database SQL",
466:             ),
467:         ]
468: 
469:         # Create peripheral chunks that each share 1-2 keywords with hub
470:         keywords_sets = [
471:             "Python programming basics",
472:             "asyncio tutorial guide",
473:             "FastAPI framework docs",
474:             "React components state",
475:             "JavaScript fundamentals",
476:             "TypeScript types interfaces",
477:             "database queries optimization",
478:             "SQL syntax reference",
479:         ]
480: 
481:         for i, keywords in enumerate(keywords_sets):
482:             chunks.append(
483:                 TemporalChunk(
484:                     id=uuid4(),
485:                     namespace_id=uuid4(),
486:                     document_id=uuid4(),
487:                     content=keywords,
488:                 )
489:             )
490: 
491:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
492:         core_ids = indexer.build_skeleton()
493: 
494:         # Hub chunk should be core (highest connectivity)
495:         assert hub_id in core_ids
496: 
497:     def test_isolated_chunks_not_core(self):
498:         """Test that isolated chunks with unique keywords are not core."""
499:         indexer = SkeletonIndexer(core_ratio=0.3)
500: 
501:         # Create connected cluster
502:         cluster_chunks = [
503:             TemporalChunk(
504:                 id=uuid4(),
505:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
506:                 document_id=uuid4(),
507:                 content="Python asyncio programming concurrent",
508:             ),
509:             TemporalChunk(
510:                 id=uuid4(),
511:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
512:                 document_id=uuid4(),
513:                 content="Python asyncio tutorial examples",
514:             ),
515:             TemporalChunk(
516:                 id=uuid4(),
517:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
518:                 document_id=uuid4(),
519:                 content="Python concurrent programming patterns",
520:             ),
521:         ]
522: 
523:         # Create isolated chunk with unique keywords
524:         isolated_id = uuid4()
525:         isolated_chunk = TemporalChunk(
526:             id=isolated_id,
527:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
528:             document_id=uuid4(),
529:             content="Xyzzy foobar unique unrelated content",
530:         )
531: 
532:         indexer.add_chunks_batch(cluster_chunks + [isolated_chunk])
533:         core_ids = indexer.build_skeleton()
534: 
535:         # Isolated chunk should NOT be core
536:         assert isolated_id not in core_ids
````

````python
  1: """Unit tests for temporal edge storage."""
  2: 
  3: from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
  4: from uuid import uuid4

  6: from khora.engines.skeleton.temporal_edges import TemporalEdge

  8: 
  9: class TestTemporalEdge:
 10:     """Tests for TemporalEdge dataclass."""
 11: 
 12:     def test_create_temporal_edge(self):
 13:         """Test creating a temporal edge."""
 14:         edge_id = uuid4()
 15:         namespace_id = uuid4()
 16:         source_id = uuid4()
 17:         target_id = uuid4()
 18:         occurred_at = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC)
 19: 
 20:         edge = TemporalEdge(
 21:             id=edge_id,
 22:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 23:             source_entity_id=source_id,
 24:             target_entity_id=target_id,
 25:             relationship_type="WORKS_FOR",
 26:             description="Alice works for Acme Corp",
 27:             occurred_at=occurred_at,
 28:             confidence=0.95,
 29:         )
 30: 
 31:         assert edge.id == edge_id
 32:         assert edge.namespace_id == namespace_id
 33:         assert edge.source_entity_id == source_id
 34:         assert edge.target_entity_id == target_id
 35:         assert edge.relationship_type == "WORKS_FOR"
 36:         assert edge.description == "Alice works for Acme Corp"
 37:         assert edge.occurred_at == occurred_at
 38:         assert edge.confidence == 0.95
 39:         assert edge.is_valid is True
 40: 
 41:     def test_edge_defaults(self):
 42:         """Test temporal edge default values."""
 43:         edge = TemporalEdge(
 44:             id=uuid4(),
 45:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
 46:             source_entity_id=uuid4(),
 47:             target_entity_id=uuid4(),
 48:             relationship_type="RELATES_TO",
 49:         )
 50: 
 51:         assert edge.description == ""
 52:         assert edge.is_valid is True
 53:         assert edge.invalidated_by_id is None
 54:         assert edge.invalidation_reason is None
 55:         assert edge.confidence == 1.0
 56:         assert edge.properties == {}
 57:         assert edge.source_document_ids == []
 58:         assert edge.source_chunk_ids == []
 59: 
 60:     def test_edge_with_validity_window(self):
 61:         """Test edge with temporal validity window."""
 62:         valid_from = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
 63:         valid_until = datetime(2024, 12, 31, tzinfo=UTC)
 64: 
 65:         edge = TemporalEdge(
 66:             id=uuid4(),
 67:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
 68:             source_entity_id=uuid4(),
 69:             target_entity_id=uuid4(),
 70:             relationship_type="CEO_OF",
 71:             valid_from=valid_from,
 72:             valid_until=valid_until,
 73:         )
 74: 
 75:         assert edge.valid_from == valid_from
 76:         assert edge.valid_until == valid_until

 78:     def test_edge_invalidation(self):
 79:         """Test edge invalidation tracking."""
 80:         original_edge_id = uuid4()
 81:         new_edge_id = uuid4()
 82: 
 83:         edge = TemporalEdge(
 84:             id=original_edge_id,
 85:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
 86:             source_entity_id=uuid4(),
 87:             target_entity_id=uuid4(),
 88:             relationship_type="WORKS_FOR",
 89:             is_valid=False,
 90:             invalidated_by_id=new_edge_id,
 91:             invalidation_reason="Superseded by newer WORKS_FOR edge",
 92:         )
 93: 
 94:         assert edge.is_valid is False
 95:         assert edge.invalidated_by_id == new_edge_id
 96:         assert "Superseded" in edge.invalidation_reason

 98:     def test_edge_with_sources(self):

119: class TestBiTemporalModel:
120:     """Tests for bi-temporal model concepts."""
121: 
122:     def test_occurrence_vs_ingestion_time(self):
123:         """Test that occurrence and ingestion times are tracked separately."""
124:         # Event happened on June 1
125:         occurred_at = datetime(2024, 6, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
126:         # We learned about it on June 15
127:         ingested_at = datetime(2024, 6, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
128: 
129:         edge = TemporalEdge(
130:             id=uuid4(),
131:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
132:             source_entity_id=uuid4(),
133:             target_entity_id=uuid4(),
134:             relationship_type="ACQUIRED",
135:             occurred_at=occurred_at,
136:             ingested_at=ingested_at,
137:         )
138: 
139:         assert edge.occurred_at == occurred_at
140:         assert edge.ingested_at == ingested_at
141:         assert edge.occurred_at < edge.ingested_at
142: 
143:     def test_validity_window_semantics(self):
144:         """Test validity window semantics."""
145:         # Alice was CEO from Jan 1 to June 30
146:         valid_from = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
147:         valid_until = datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
148: 
149:         edge = TemporalEdge(
150:             id=uuid4(),
151:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
152:             source_entity_id=uuid4(),  # Alice
153:             target_entity_id=uuid4(),  # Company
154:             relationship_type="CEO_OF",
155:             occurred_at=valid_from,  # When we first knew about it
156:             valid_from=valid_from,
157:             valid_until=valid_until,
158:         )
159: 
160:         # Check if a point in time is within validity window
161:         def is_valid_at(edge: TemporalEdge, t: datetime) -> bool:
162:             if edge.valid_from and t < edge.valid_from:
163:                 return False
164:             if edge.valid_until and t >= edge.valid_until:
165:                 return False
166:             return edge.is_valid
167: 
168:         # Before validity
169:         assert not is_valid_at(edge, datetime(2023, 12, 31, tzinfo=UTC))
170: 
171:         # During validity
172:         assert is_valid_at(edge, datetime(2024, 3, 15, tzinfo=UTC))
173: 
174:         # After validity
175:         assert not is_valid_at(edge, datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=UTC))
176:

178: class TestEdgeConflictDetection:
179:     """Tests for edge conflict detection logic."""
180: 
181:     def test_exclusive_relationship_types(self):
182:         """Test identification of mutually exclusive relationship types."""
183:         exclusive_types = {
184:             "WORKS_FOR",
185:             "REPORTS_TO",
186:             "MANAGES",
187:             "MARRIED_TO",
188:             "CEO_OF",
189:             "PRESIDENT_OF",
190:             "LOCATED_AT",
191:             "HEADQUARTERED_IN",
192:         }
193: 
194:         # WORKS_FOR is exclusive (one employer at a time)
195:         assert "WORKS_FOR" in exclusive_types
196: 
197:         # KNOWS is not exclusive (can know many people)
198:         assert "KNOWS" not in exclusive_types
199: 
200:     def test_conflict_detection_newer_wins(self):
201:         """Test that newer edge invalidates older for exclusive types."""
202:         source_id = uuid4()
203:         target_id = uuid4()
204:         namespace_id = uuid4()
205: 
206:         # Old edge: Alice works for OldCorp (June 2023)
207:         old_edge = TemporalEdge(
208:             id=uuid4(),
209:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
210:             source_entity_id=source_id,
211:             target_entity_id=target_id,
212:             relationship_type="WORKS_FOR",
213:             occurred_at=datetime(2023, 6, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
214:         )
215: 
216:         # New edge: Alice works for NewCorp (Jan 2024)
217:         new_edge = TemporalEdge(
218:             id=uuid4(),
219:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
220:             source_entity_id=source_id,
221:             target_entity_id=uuid4(),  # Different target
222:             relationship_type="WORKS_FOR",
223:             occurred_at=datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
224:         )
225: 
226:         # New edge is more recent
227:         assert new_edge.occurred_at > old_edge.occurred_at
228: 
229:         # Both involve same source and relationship type
230:         assert old_edge.source_entity_id == new_edge.source_entity_id
231:         assert old_edge.relationship_type == new_edge.relationship_type
232:

234: class TestTemporalQueryPatterns:
235:     """Tests for common temporal query patterns."""
236: 
237:     def test_point_in_time_query(self):
238:         """Test finding edges valid at a specific point in time."""
239:         edges = [
240:             TemporalEdge(
241:                 id=uuid4(),
242:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
243:                 source_entity_id=uuid4(),
244:                 target_entity_id=uuid4(),
245:                 relationship_type="CEO_OF",
246:                 occurred_at=datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
247:                 valid_from=datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
248:                 valid_until=datetime(2024, 6, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
249:             ),
250:             TemporalEdge(
251:                 id=uuid4(),
252:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
253:                 source_entity_id=uuid4(),
254:                 target_entity_id=uuid4(),
255:                 relationship_type="CEO_OF",
256:                 occurred_at=datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
257:                 valid_from=datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
258:                 valid_until=None,  # Still valid
259:             ),
260:         ]
261: 
262:         # Query: Who was CEO on March 15, 2024?
263:         query_time = datetime(2024, 3, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
264:         valid_edges = [
265:             e
266:             for e in edges
267:             if (e.valid_from is None or e.valid_from <= query_time)
268:             and (e.valid_until is None or e.valid_until > query_time)
269:         ]
270:         assert len(valid_edges) == 1
271:         assert valid_edges[0].occurred_at == datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
272: 
273:         # Query: Who is CEO on August 1, 2024?
274:         query_time = datetime(2024, 8, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
275:         valid_edges = [
276:             e
277:             for e in edges
278:             if (e.valid_from is None or e.valid_from <= query_time)
279:             and (e.valid_until is None or e.valid_until > query_time)
280:         ]
281:         assert len(valid_edges) == 1
282:         assert valid_edges[0].occurred_at == datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
283: 
284:     def test_time_range_query(self):
285:         """Test finding edges within a time range."""
286:         edges = [
287:             TemporalEdge(
288:                 id=uuid4(),
289:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
290:                 source_entity_id=uuid4(),
291:                 target_entity_id=uuid4(),
292:                 relationship_type="MEETING",
293:                 occurred_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, tzinfo=UTC),
294:             ),
295:             TemporalEdge(
296:                 id=uuid4(),
297:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
298:                 source_entity_id=uuid4(),
299:                 target_entity_id=uuid4(),
300:                 relationship_type="MEETING",
301:                 occurred_at=datetime(2024, 2, 10, tzinfo=UTC),
302:             ),
303:             TemporalEdge(
304:                 id=uuid4(),
305:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
306:                 source_entity_id=uuid4(),
307:                 target_entity_id=uuid4(),
308:                 relationship_type="MEETING",
309:                 occurred_at=datetime(2024, 3, 5, tzinfo=UTC),
310:             ),
311:         ]
312: 
313:         # Query: Meetings in February 2024
314:         start = datetime(2024, 2, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
315:         end = datetime(2024, 3, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
316: 
317:         matching = [e for e in edges if start <= e.occurred_at < end]
318:         assert len(matching) == 1
319:         assert matching[0].occurred_at == datetime(2024, 2, 10, tzinfo=UTC)
320: 
321:     def test_relative_time_query(self):
322:         """Test relative time queries like 'yesterday'."""
323:         now = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, tzinfo=UTC)
324: 
325:         edges = [
326:             TemporalEdge(
327:                 id=uuid4(),
328:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
329:                 source_entity_id=uuid4(),
330:                 target_entity_id=uuid4(),
331:                 relationship_type="SENT_MESSAGE",
332:                 occurred_at=datetime(2024, 6, 14, 10, 0, tzinfo=UTC),  # Yesterday
333:             ),
334:             TemporalEdge(
335:                 id=uuid4(),
336:                 namespace_id=uuid4(),
337:                 source_entity_id=uuid4(),
338:                 target_entity_id=uuid4(),
339:                 relationship_type="SENT_MESSAGE",
340:                 occurred_at=datetime(2024, 6, 15, 9, 0, tzinfo=UTC),  # Today
341:             ),
342:         ]
343: 
344:         # "Yesterday" relative to now
345:         yesterday_start = (now - timedelta(days=1)).replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
346:         yesterday_end = now.replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
347: 
348:         matching = [e for e in edges if yesterday_start <= e.occurred_at < yesterday_end]
349:         assert len(matching) == 1
350:         assert matching[0].occurred_at.day == 14
````

## File: tests/unit/engines/test_time_hierarchy.py
````python
  1: """Unit tests for the time hierarchy builder."""
  2: 
  3: from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
  4: from uuid import uuid4
  5: 
  6: import pytest
  7: 
  8: from khora.db.models import TimeGranularity
  9: from khora.engines.skeleton.time_hierarchy import TimeNode
 10: 
 11: 
 12: class TestTimeNode:
 13:     """Tests for TimeNode dataclass."""
 14: 
 15:     def test_create_time_node(self):
 16:         """Test creating a time node."""
 17:         node_id = uuid4()
 18:         namespace_id = uuid4()
 19:         start = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
 20:         end = datetime(2024, 2, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
 21: 
 22:         node = TimeNode(
 23:             id=node_id,
 24:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 25:             granularity=TimeGranularity.MONTH,
 26:             start_time=start,
 27:             end_time=end,
 28:             parent_id=None,
 29:             name="January 2024",
 30:             edge_count=5,
 31:             entity_count=10,
 32:         )
 33: 
 34:         assert node.id == node_id
 35:         assert node.namespace_id == namespace_id
 36:         assert node.granularity == TimeGranularity.MONTH
 37:         assert node.start_time == start
 38:         assert node.end_time == end
 39:         assert node.parent_id is None
 40:         assert node.name == "January 2024"
 41:         assert node.edge_count == 5
 42:         assert node.entity_count == 10
 43: 
 44:     def test_time_node_defaults(self):
 45:         """Test time node default values."""
 46:         node = TimeNode(
 47:             id=uuid4(),
 48:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
 49:             granularity=TimeGranularity.DAY,
 50:             start_time=datetime.now(UTC),
 51:             end_time=datetime.now(UTC),
 52:             parent_id=None,
 53:             name="test",
 54:         )
 55: 
 56:         assert node.edge_count == 0
 57:         assert node.entity_count == 0
 58: 
 59: 
 60: class TestTimeGranularity:
 61:     """Tests for time granularity constants."""
 62: 
 63:     def test_granularity_values(self):
 64:         """Test granularity constant values."""
 65:         assert TimeGranularity.YEAR == "year"
 66:         assert TimeGranularity.QUARTER == "quarter"
 67:         assert TimeGranularity.MONTH == "month"
 68:         assert TimeGranularity.WEEK == "week"
 69:         assert TimeGranularity.DAY == "day"
 70: 
 71: 
 72: @pytest.mark.unit
 73: class TestTimeHierarchyLogic:
 74:     """Tests for time hierarchy calculation logic."""
 75: 
 76:     def test_quarter_calculation(self):
 77:         """Test quarter calculation from dates."""
 78:         # Q1: Jan, Feb, Mar
 79:         for month in [1, 2, 3]:
 80:             dt = datetime(2024, month, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
 81:             quarter = (dt.month - 1) // 3 + 1
 82:             assert quarter == 1
 83: 
 84:         # Q2: Apr, May, Jun
 85:         for month in [4, 5, 6]:
 86:             dt = datetime(2024, month, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
 87:             quarter = (dt.month - 1) // 3 + 1
 88:             assert quarter == 2
 89: 
 90:         # Q3: Jul, Aug, Sep
 91:         for month in [7, 8, 9]:
 92:             dt = datetime(2024, month, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
 93:             quarter = (dt.month - 1) // 3 + 1
 94:             assert quarter == 3
 95: 
 96:         # Q4: Oct, Nov, Dec
 97:         for month in [10, 11, 12]:
 98:             dt = datetime(2024, month, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
 99:             quarter = (dt.month - 1) // 3 + 1
100:             assert quarter == 4
101: 
102:     def test_quarter_start_calculation(self):
103:         """Test quarter start month calculation."""
104:         for quarter in [1, 2, 3, 4]:
105:             start_month = (quarter - 1) * 3 + 1
106:             expected_starts = {1: 1, 2: 4, 3: 7, 4: 10}
107:             assert start_month == expected_starts[quarter]
108: 
109:     def test_iso_week_calculation(self):
110:         """Test ISO week calculation."""
111:         # 2024-01-01 is Monday of week 1
112:         dt = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
113:         iso = dt.isocalendar()
114:         assert iso.year == 2024
115:         assert iso.week == 1
116: 
117:         # 2024-01-08 is Monday of week 2
118:         dt = datetime(2024, 1, 8, tzinfo=UTC)
119:         iso = dt.isocalendar()
120:         assert iso.year == 2024
121:         assert iso.week == 2
122: 
123:     def test_day_boundaries(self):
124:         """Test day start/end boundaries."""
125:         dt = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=UTC)
126: 
127:         # Normalize to start of day
128:         day_start = dt.replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
129:         assert day_start.hour == 0
130:         assert day_start.minute == 0
131:         assert day_start.second == 0
132: 
133:         # End of day is start of next day
134:         day_end = day_start + timedelta(days=1)
135:         assert day_end.day == 16
136: 
137: 
138: class TestTimeNodeNaming:
139:     """Tests for time node naming conventions."""
140: 
141:     def test_year_name(self):
142:         """Test year node naming."""
143:         dt = datetime(2024, 6, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
144:         name = str(dt.year)
145:         assert name == "2024"
146: 
147:     def test_quarter_name(self):
148:         """Test quarter node naming."""
149:         for month, expected_q in [(1, 1), (4, 2), (7, 3), (10, 4)]:
150:             dt = datetime(2024, month, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
151:             quarter = (dt.month - 1) // 3 + 1
152:             name = f"Q{quarter} {dt.year}"
153:             assert name == f"Q{expected_q} 2024"
154: 
155:     def test_month_name(self):
156:         """Test month node naming."""
157:         dt = datetime(2024, 1, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
158:         name = dt.strftime("%B %Y")
159:         assert name == "January 2024"
160: 
161:         dt = datetime(2024, 12, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
162:         name = dt.strftime("%B %Y")
163:         assert name == "December 2024"
164: 
165:     def test_week_name(self):
166:         """Test week node naming."""
167:         dt = datetime(2024, 1, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
168:         iso = dt.isocalendar()
169:         name = f"Week {iso.week} {iso.year}"
170:         assert "Week" in name
171:         assert "2024" in name
172: 
173:     def test_day_name(self):
174:         """Test day node naming."""
175:         dt = datetime(2024, 1, 15, tzinfo=UTC)
176:         name = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
177:         assert name == "2024-01-15"
178: 
179: 
180: class TestTimeHierarchyIntegrity:
181:     """Tests for time hierarchy integrity checks."""
182: 
183:     def test_year_contains_quarters(self):
184:         """Test that year properly contains all quarters."""
185:         year_start = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
186:         year_end = datetime(2025, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
187: 
188:         # All quarter starts should be within year
189:         q_starts = [
190:             datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
191:             datetime(2024, 4, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
192:             datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
193:             datetime(2024, 10, 1, tzinfo=UTC),
194:         ]
195: 
196:         for q_start in q_starts:
197:             assert year_start <= q_start < year_end
198: 
199:     def test_quarter_contains_months(self):
200:         """Test that quarter properly contains all months."""
201:         # Q1 2024
202:         q_start = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
203:         q_end = datetime(2024, 4, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
204: 
205:         months = [1, 2, 3]
206:         for month in months:
207:             m_start = datetime(2024, month, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
208:             assert q_start <= m_start < q_end
209: 
210:     def test_month_contains_weeks(self):
211:         """Test that month contains weeks (partial overlap is ok)."""
212:         # January 2024
213:         m_start = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
214: 
215:         # Week 1 starts on 2024-01-01
216:         w1_start = datetime.fromisocalendar(2024, 1, 1).replace(tzinfo=UTC)
217:         assert w1_start >= m_start
218: 
219:     def test_no_time_gaps(self):
220:         """Test that hierarchy has no gaps."""
221:         # Year end == next year start
222:         year1_end = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
223:         year2_start = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
224:         assert year1_end == year2_start
225: 
226:         # Quarter end == next quarter start
227:         q1_end = datetime(2024, 4, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
228:         q2_start = datetime(2024, 4, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
229:         assert q1_end == q2_start
````

````python
 1: """Tests for API module."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: import pytest
 6: from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
 7: 
 8: import khora
 9: 
10: 
11: @pytest.mark.unit
12: class TestStatusEndpoints:
13:     """Tests for status check endpoints."""
14: 
15:     def test_status_check(self, test_client: TestClient) -> None:
16:         """Test basic status check endpoint."""
17:         response = test_client.get("/status")
18: 
19:         assert response.status_code == 200
20:         data = response.json()
21:         assert data["status"] == "ok"
22:         assert "timestamp" in data
23:         assert data["version"] == khora.__version__
24:         assert data["service"] == "khora"
25: 
26:     def test_health_check(self, test_client: TestClient) -> None:
27:         """Test health check endpoint."""
28:         response = test_client.get("/health")
29: 
30:         assert response.status_code == 200
31:         data = response.json()
32:         assert data["status"] == "healthy"
33:         assert "timestamp" in data
34:         assert data["version"] == khora.__version__
35: 
36:     def test_readiness_check(self, test_client: TestClient) -> None:
37:         """Test readiness check endpoint."""
38:         response = test_client.get("/health/ready")
39: 
40:         assert response.status_code == 200
41:         data = response.json()
42:         assert data["status"] in ["ready", "not_ready"]
43:         assert "timestamp" in data
44:         assert "checks" in data
45: 
46:     def test_liveness_check(self, test_client: TestClient) -> None:
47:         """Test liveness check endpoint."""
48:         response = test_client.get("/health/live")
49: 
50:         assert response.status_code == 200
51:         data = response.json()
52:         assert data["status"] == "alive"
53:         assert "timestamp" in data
54: 
55: 
56: @pytest.mark.unit
57: class TestConfig:
58:     """Tests for configuration."""

61:         """Test default configuration values."""

63: 
64:         config = KhoraConfig()
65:         assert config.app_name == "khora"
66:         assert config.environment == "development"
67:         assert config.debug is False
68:         assert config.api_host == "127.0.0.1"
69:         assert config.api_port == 8000
70:         assert config.auth_enabled is True
71: 
72:     def test_config_from_env(self, monkeypatch) -> None:
73:         """Test configuration from environment variables."""
74:         from khora.config import KhoraConfig
75: 
76:         monkeypatch.setenv("KHORA_DEBUG", "true")
77:         monkeypatch.setenv("KHORA_API_PORT", "9000")
78:         monkeypatch.setenv("KHORA_ENVIRONMENT", "staging")
79: 
80:         config = KhoraConfig()
81:         assert config.debug is True
82:         assert config.api_port == 9000
83:         assert config.environment == "staging"
````

## File: tests/unit/test_config_backends.py
````python
  1: """Tests for discriminated-union backend config parsing and backwards compatibility."""
  2: 
  3: import pytest
  4: 
  5: from khora.config.schema import (
  6:     ArcadeDBGraphConfig,
  7:     ArcadeDBVectorConfig,
  8:     KhoraConfig,
  9:     KuzuConfig,
 10:     MemgraphConfig,
 11:     Neo4jConfig,
 12:     PgVectorConfig,
 13:     StorageSettings,
 14: )
 15: 
 16: 
 17: @pytest.mark.unit
 18: class TestStorageSettingsBackwardsCompat:
 19:     """Legacy flat fields should still work and be migrated to new-style configs."""
 20: 
 21:     def test_legacy_neo4j_fields_migrated(self):
 22:         settings = StorageSettings(
 23:             neo4j_url="bolt://localhost:7687",
 24:             neo4j_user="admin",
 25:             neo4j_password="secret",
 26:             neo4j_database="mydb",
 27:         )
 28:         assert isinstance(settings.graph, Neo4jConfig)
 29:         assert settings.graph.url == "bolt://localhost:7687"
 30:         assert settings.graph.user == "admin"
 31:         assert settings.graph.password == "secret"
 32:         assert settings.graph.database == "mydb"
 33: 
 34:     def test_legacy_pgvector_fields_migrated(self):
 35:         settings = StorageSettings(
 36:             pgvector_url="postgresql://localhost:5432/vectors",
 37:             embedding_dimension=768,
 38:         )
 39:         assert isinstance(settings.vector, PgVectorConfig)
 40:         assert settings.vector.url == "postgresql://localhost:5432/vectors"
 41:         assert settings.vector.embedding_dimension == 768
 42: 
 43:     def test_new_style_graph_config_takes_precedence(self):
 44:         settings = StorageSettings(
 45:             neo4j_url="bolt://old:7687",
 46:             graph=KuzuConfig(database_path="/tmp/kuzu"),
 47:         )
 48:         assert isinstance(settings.graph, KuzuConfig)
 49:         assert settings.graph.database_path == "/tmp/kuzu"
 50: 
 51:     def test_new_style_vector_config_takes_precedence(self):
 52:         settings = StorageSettings(
 53:             pgvector_url="postgresql://old:5432/db",
 54:             vector=ArcadeDBVectorConfig(url="http://localhost:2480"),
 55:         )
 56:         assert isinstance(settings.vector, ArcadeDBVectorConfig)
 57:         assert settings.vector.url == "http://localhost:2480"
 58: 
 59:     def test_defaults(self):
 60:         settings = StorageSettings()
 61:         # Graph backend is optional - None by default
 62:         assert settings.graph is None
 63:         assert isinstance(settings.vector, PgVectorConfig)
 64:         assert settings.vector.url is None
 65: 
 66: 
 67: @pytest.mark.unit
 68: class TestDiscriminatedUnionParsing:
 69:     """Discriminated union configs parsed from dict/YAML."""
 70: 
 71:     def test_kuzu_config_from_dict(self):
 72:         settings = StorageSettings.model_validate(
 73:             {
 74:                 "graph": {"backend": "kuzu", "database_path": "/data/kuzu"},
 75:             }
 76:         )
 77:         assert isinstance(settings.graph, KuzuConfig)
 78:         assert settings.graph.database_path == "/data/kuzu"
 79: 
 80:     def test_memgraph_config_from_dict(self):
 81:         settings = StorageSettings.model_validate(
 82:             {
 83:                 "graph": {"backend": "memgraph", "url": "bolt://mg:7687"},
 84:             }
 85:         )
 86:         assert isinstance(settings.graph, MemgraphConfig)
 87:         assert settings.graph.url == "bolt://mg:7687"
 88: 
 89:     def test_arcadedb_graph_config_from_dict(self):
 90:         settings = StorageSettings.model_validate(
 91:             {
 92:                 "graph": {"backend": "arcadedb", "url": "http://arcade:2480"},
 93:             }
 94:         )
 95:         assert isinstance(settings.graph, ArcadeDBGraphConfig)
 96:         assert settings.graph.url == "http://arcade:2480"
 97: 
 98:     def test_arcadedb_vector_config_from_dict(self):
 99:         settings = StorageSettings.model_validate(
100:             {
101:                 "vector": {"backend": "arcadedb", "url": "http://arcade:2480", "embedding_dimension": 768},
102:             }
103:         )
104:         assert isinstance(settings.vector, ArcadeDBVectorConfig)
105:         assert settings.vector.embedding_dimension == 768
106: 
107:     def test_neo4j_is_default_graph_backend(self):
108:         settings = StorageSettings.model_validate(
109:             {
110:                 "graph": {"backend": "neo4j", "url": "bolt://neo:7687"},
111:             }
112:         )
113:         assert isinstance(settings.graph, Neo4jConfig)
114: 
115:     def test_pgvector_is_default_vector_backend(self):
116:         settings = StorageSettings.model_validate(
117:             {
118:                 "vector": {"backend": "pgvector", "url": "postgresql://pg:5432/db"},
119:             }
120:         )
121:         assert isinstance(settings.vector, PgVectorConfig)
122: 
123: 
124: @pytest.mark.unit
125: class TestKhoraConfigGraphHelpers:
126:     """KhoraConfig.get_graph_config() / get_vector_config() work correctly."""
127: 
128:     def test_get_graph_config_neo4j(self):
129:         config = KhoraConfig(
130:             neo4j_url="bolt://neo4j:pass@localhost:7687",
131:         )
132:         graph = config.get_graph_config()
133:         assert isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig)
134:         assert graph.url == "bolt://localhost:7687"
135:         assert graph.user == "neo4j"
136:         assert graph.password == "pass"
137: 
138:     def test_get_graph_config_kuzu(self):
139:         config = KhoraConfig(
140:             storage=StorageSettings(
141:                 graph=KuzuConfig(database_path="/tmp/kuzu_test"),
142:             ),
143:         )
144:         graph = config.get_graph_config()
145:         assert isinstance(graph, KuzuConfig)
146:         assert graph.database_path == "/tmp/kuzu_test"
147: 
148:     def test_get_vector_config_defaults_to_postgresql_url(self):
149:         config = KhoraConfig(
150:             database_url="postgresql://localhost:5432/khora",
151:         )
152:         vector = config.get_vector_config()
153:         assert isinstance(vector, PgVectorConfig)
154:         assert vector.url == "postgresql://localhost:5432/khora"
155: 
156: 
157: @pytest.mark.unit
158: class TestArcadeDBDualRole:
159:     """ArcadeDB dual-role detection."""
160: 
161:     def test_both_graph_and_vector_arcadedb(self):
162:         settings = StorageSettings.model_validate(
163:             {
164:                 "graph": {"backend": "arcadedb", "url": "http://localhost:2480"},
165:                 "vector": {"backend": "arcadedb", "url": "http://localhost:2480"},
166:             }
167:         )
168:         assert isinstance(settings.graph, ArcadeDBGraphConfig)
169:         assert isinstance(settings.vector, ArcadeDBVectorConfig)
170:         assert settings.graph.url == settings.vector.url
````

## File: tests/unit/test_entity_resolution.py
````python
  1: """Unit tests for extraction/entity_resolution.py — Entity resolution."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock
  6: from uuid import uuid4
  7: 
  8: import pytest
  9: 
 10: from khora.core.models.entity import Entity, EntityType
 11: from khora.extraction.entity_resolution import (
 12:     EntityResolver,
 13:     ResolutionCandidate,
 14:     ResolutionResult,
 15:     resolve_and_merge_entity,
 16: )
 17: 
 18: 
 19: def _make_entity(name: str = "Test", entity_type: EntityType = EntityType.PERSON, **kwargs) -> Entity:
 20:     """Helper to create an Entity with sensible defaults."""
 21:     return Entity(
 22:         namespace_id=kwargs.get("namespace_id", uuid4()),
 23:         name=name,
 24:         entity_type=entity_type,
 25:         description=kwargs.get("description", ""),
 26:         attributes=kwargs.get("attributes", {}),
 27:         metadata=kwargs.get("metadata", {}),
 28:     )
 29: 
 30: 
 31: class TestResolutionCandidate:
 32:     """Tests for ResolutionCandidate dataclass."""
 33: 
 34:     def test_create(self) -> None:
 35:         """Basic creation."""
 36:         entity = _make_entity()
 37:         c = ResolutionCandidate(entity=entity, match_type="exact", score=1.0)
 38:         assert c.entity is entity
 39:         assert c.match_type == "exact"
 40:         assert c.score == 1.0
 41: 
 42: 
 43: class TestResolutionResult:
 44:     """Tests for ResolutionResult dataclass."""
 45: 
 46:     def test_no_match(self) -> None:
 47:         """No match defaults."""
 48:         r = ResolutionResult(is_duplicate=False)
 49:         assert r.is_duplicate is False
 50:         assert r.existing_entity is None
 51:         assert r.should_merge is False
 52: 
 53:     def test_match(self) -> None:
 54:         """Match with existing entity."""
 55:         entity = _make_entity()
 56:         r = ResolutionResult(
 57:             is_duplicate=True,
 58:             existing_entity=entity,
 59:             match_type="exact",
 60:             match_score=1.0,
 61:             should_merge=True,
 62:         )
 63:         assert r.is_duplicate is True
 64:         assert r.should_merge is True
 65: 
 66: 
 67: class TestEntityResolver:
 68:     """Tests for EntityResolver."""
 69: 
 70:     def _make_resolver(self, entities: list[Entity] | None = None) -> tuple[EntityResolver, MagicMock]:
 71:         """Create resolver with mock storage."""
 72:         storage = MagicMock()
 73:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=entities or [])
 74:         resolver = EntityResolver(storage, embedder=None)
 75:         return resolver, storage
 76: 
 77:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 78:     async def test_exact_match(self) -> None:
 79:         """Exact name match returns duplicate."""
 80:         ns_id = uuid4()
 81:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
 82:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
 83: 
 84:         result = await resolver.resolve("Alice", "PERSON", ns_id)
 85:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
 86:         assert result.match_type == "exact"
 87:         assert result.match_score == 1.0
 88: 
 89:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 90:     async def test_exact_match_case_insensitive(self) -> None:
 91:         """Exact match is case-insensitive."""
 92:         ns_id = uuid4()
 93:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
 94:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
 95: 
 96:         result = await resolver.resolve("alice", "PERSON", ns_id)
 97:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
 98:         assert result.match_type == "exact"
 99: 
100:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
101:     async def test_alias_match(self) -> None:
102:         """Alias matching finds entities by their aliases."""
103:         ns_id = uuid4()
104:         existing = _make_entity("Robert", namespace_id=ns_id, metadata={"aliases": ["Bob"]})
105:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
106: 
107:         result = await resolver.resolve("Bob", "PERSON", ns_id)
108:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
109:         assert result.match_type == "alias"
110: 
111:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
112:     async def test_alias_match_reverse(self) -> None:
113:         """New entity alias matches existing entity name."""
114:         ns_id = uuid4()
115:         existing = _make_entity("Bob", namespace_id=ns_id)
116:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
117: 
118:         result = await resolver.resolve("Robert", "PERSON", ns_id, aliases=["Bob"])
119:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
120:         assert result.match_type == "alias"
121: 
122:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
123:     async def test_fuzzy_match(self) -> None:
124:         """Fuzzy match catches near-identical names."""
125:         ns_id = uuid4()
126:         existing = _make_entity("Alexander Hamilton", namespace_id=ns_id)
127:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
128:         resolver._fuzzy_threshold = 0.8
129: 
130:         result = await resolver.resolve("Alexandr Hamilton", "PERSON", ns_id)
131:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
132:         assert result.match_type == "fuzzy"
133: 
134:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
135:     async def test_no_match(self) -> None:
136:         """No match returns non-duplicate."""
137:         ns_id = uuid4()
138:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
139:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
140: 
141:         result = await resolver.resolve("Completely Different", "PERSON", ns_id)
142:         assert result.is_duplicate is False
143: 
144:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
145:     async def test_exact_match_prioritized(self) -> None:
146:         """Exact match short-circuits before fuzzy/embedding."""
147:         ns_id = uuid4()
148:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
149:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
150: 
151:         result = await resolver.resolve("Alice", "PERSON", ns_id)
152:         # Should have returned immediately with exact match
153:         assert result.match_type == "exact"
154: 
155:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
156:     async def test_disabled_strategies(self) -> None:
157:         """Disabling strategies skips them."""
158:         ns_id = uuid4()
159:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
160:         storage = MagicMock()
161:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
162: 
163:         resolver = EntityResolver(
164:             storage,
165:             exact_match=False,
166:             alias_match=False,
167:             fuzzy_match=False,
168:             embedding_match=False,
169:         )
170:         result = await resolver.resolve("Alice", "PERSON", ns_id)
171:         assert result.is_duplicate is False
172: 
173:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
174:     async def test_cache_populated(self) -> None:
175:         """Entity cache is populated on first resolve call."""
176:         ns_id = uuid4()
177:         resolver, storage = self._make_resolver([])
178: 
179:         await resolver.resolve("Test", "PERSON", ns_id)
180:         # Cache should have been populated
181:         cache_key = f"{ns_id}:PERSON"
182:         assert cache_key in resolver._entity_cache
183: 
184:     def test_invalidate_cache_all(self) -> None:
185:         """invalidate_cache with no namespace clears everything."""
186:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver()
187:         resolver._entity_cache["key1"] = []
188:         resolver._entity_cache["key2"] = []
189:         resolver.invalidate_cache()
190:         assert len(resolver._entity_cache) == 0
191: 
192:     def test_invalidate_cache_namespace(self) -> None:
193:         """invalidate_cache with namespace only clears matching entries."""
194:         ns_id = uuid4()
195:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver()
196:         resolver._entity_cache[f"{ns_id}:PERSON"] = []
197:         resolver._entity_cache["other:PERSON"] = []
198:         resolver.invalidate_cache(namespace_id=ns_id)
199:         assert f"{ns_id}:PERSON" not in resolver._entity_cache
200:         assert "other:PERSON" in resolver._entity_cache
201: 
202: 
203: class TestResolveAndMergeEntity:
204:     """Tests for the resolve_and_merge_entity convenience function."""
205: 
206:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
207:     async def test_high_score_merge(self) -> None:
208:         """High-score match merges into existing entity."""
209:         ns_id = uuid4()
210:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
211:         existing.mention_count = 1
212: 
213:         storage = MagicMock()
214:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
215: 
216:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
217:             "Alice",
218:             "PERSON",
219:             ns_id,
220:             storage,
221:             description="A person named Alice",
222:             attributes={"role": "engineer"},
223:         )
224:         assert is_new is False
225:         assert entity is existing
226:         assert entity.mention_count == 2
227:         assert entity.attributes.get("role") == "engineer"
228: 
229:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
230:     async def test_no_match_creates_new(self) -> None:
231:         """No match returns a new entity."""
232:         ns_id = uuid4()
233:         storage = MagicMock()
234:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
235: 
236:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
237:             "NewPerson",
238:             "PERSON",
239:             ns_id,
240:             storage,
241:             description="Brand new person",
242:         )
243:         assert is_new is True
244:         assert entity.name == "NewPerson"
245:         assert entity.entity_type == EntityType.PERSON
246:         assert entity.description == "Brand new person"
247: 
248:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
249:     async def test_merge_aliases(self) -> None:
250:         """Merging accumulates aliases."""
251:         ns_id = uuid4()
252:         existing = _make_entity("Robert", namespace_id=ns_id, metadata={"aliases": ["Bob"]})
253:         storage = MagicMock()
254:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
255: 
256:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
257:             "Robert",
258:             "PERSON",
259:             ns_id,
260:             storage,
261:             aliases=["Rob", "Bobby"],
262:         )
263:         assert is_new is False
264:         aliases = entity.metadata.get("aliases", [])
265:         assert "Bob" in aliases
266:         assert "Rob" in aliases
267:         assert "Bobby" in aliases
268: 
269:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
270:     async def test_merge_description_fill(self) -> None:
271:         """Merge fills empty description."""
272:         ns_id = uuid4()
273:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
274:         existing.description = ""
275:         storage = MagicMock()
276:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
277: 
278:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
279:             "Alice",
280:             "PERSON",
281:             ns_id,
282:             storage,
283:             description="An engineer",
284:         )
285:         assert entity.description == "An engineer"
286: 
287:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
288:     async def test_unknown_entity_type_preserved(self) -> None:
289:         """Unknown entity type is preserved as a string (not mapped to CONCEPT)."""
290:         ns_id = uuid4()
291:         storage = MagicMock()
292:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
293: 
294:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
295:             "Something",
296:             "UNKNOWN_TYPE",
297:             ns_id,
298:             storage,
299:         )
300:         assert is_new is True
301:         assert entity.entity_type == "UNKNOWN_TYPE"
````

## File: tests/unit/test_perf_optimizations.py
````python
  1: """Tests for performance optimizations (B1-B16 from PERFORMANCE_PLAN.md).
  2: 
  3: Covers:
  4: - B5: Parallel graph+vector writes in create_entity
  5: - B6: Semaphore release during retry sleep
  6: - B7: Shared embedder across documents
  7: - B11: Cache key computed once
  8: - B12: _parse_response accepts dict (no JSON round-trip)
  9: - B10: Embedding input deduplication
 10: - B2: Batch entity upsert (replaces N+1)
 11: - B8: Batch relationship storage in expansion
 12: - B9: Selective entity updates in expansion
 13: - B4: Parallel entity embedding + relationship storage
 14: - B14: Parallel entity/relationship loading in expansion
 15: """
 16: 
 17: from __future__ import annotations
 18: 
 19: import asyncio
 20: import json
 21: from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
 22: from uuid import uuid4
 23: 
 24: import pytest
 25: 
 26: from khora.core.models import Entity
 27: from khora.extraction.embedders.litellm import LiteLLMEmbedder
 28: from khora.extraction.extractors.llm import LLMEntityExtractor
 29: from khora.storage.coordinator import StorageCoordinator
 30: 
 31: # =========================================================================
 32: # B5: Parallel graph+vector writes in create_entity
 33: # =========================================================================
 34: 
 35: 
 36: class TestB5ParallelCreateEntity:
 37:     """B5: create_entity should run graph+vector writes in parallel."""
 38: 
 39:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 40:     async def test_create_entity_both_backends_called(self) -> None:
 41:         """Both graph and vector are called when both configured."""
 42:         entity = MagicMock(spec=Entity, namespace_id=uuid4())
 43:         graph = MagicMock()
 44:         graph.create_entity = AsyncMock(return_value=entity)
 45:         vec = MagicMock()
 46:         vec.create_entity = AsyncMock()
 47: 
 48:         coord = StorageCoordinator(graph=graph, vector=vec)
 49:         with patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem:
 50:             mock_telem.return_value.record_storage_op = MagicMock()
 51:             result = await coord.create_entity(entity)
 52: 
 53:         graph.create_entity.assert_awaited_once()
 54:         vec.create_entity.assert_awaited_once()
 55:         assert result is entity
 56: 
 57:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 58:     async def test_create_entity_graph_only(self) -> None:
 59:         """Works with graph backend only."""
 60:         entity = MagicMock(spec=Entity, namespace_id=uuid4())
 61:         graph = MagicMock()
 62:         graph.create_entity = AsyncMock(return_value=entity)
 63: 
 64:         coord = StorageCoordinator(graph=graph)
 65:         with patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem:
 66:             mock_telem.return_value.record_storage_op = MagicMock()
 67:             result = await coord.create_entity(entity)
 68: 
 69:         graph.create_entity.assert_awaited_once()
 70:         assert result is entity
 71: 
 72:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 73:     async def test_create_entity_vector_only(self) -> None:
 74:         """Works with vector backend only."""
 75:         entity = MagicMock(spec=Entity, namespace_id=uuid4())
 76:         vec = MagicMock()
 77:         vec.create_entity = AsyncMock()
 78: 
 79:         coord = StorageCoordinator(vector=vec)
 80:         with patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem:
 81:             mock_telem.return_value.record_storage_op = MagicMock()
 82:             await coord.create_entity(entity)
 83: 
 84:         vec.create_entity.assert_awaited_once()
 85: 
 86:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 87:     async def test_create_entity_parallel_execution(self) -> None:
 88:         """Verify graph and vector run concurrently (via asyncio.gather)."""
 89:         entity = MagicMock(spec=Entity, namespace_id=uuid4())
 90:         call_order = []
 91: 
 92:         async def slow_graph_create(e):
 93:             call_order.append("graph_start")
 94:             await asyncio.sleep(0.01)
 95:             call_order.append("graph_end")
 96:             return e
 97: 
 98:         async def slow_vec_create(e):
 99:             call_order.append("vec_start")
100:             await asyncio.sleep(0.01)
101:             call_order.append("vec_end")
102: 
103:         graph = MagicMock()
104:         graph.create_entity = slow_graph_create
105:         vec = MagicMock()
106:         vec.create_entity = slow_vec_create
107: 
108:         coord = StorageCoordinator(graph=graph, vector=vec)
109:         with patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem:
110:             mock_telem.return_value.record_storage_op = MagicMock()
111:             await coord.create_entity(entity)
112: 
113:         # Both should start before either finishes (parallel execution)
114:         assert "graph_start" in call_order
115:         assert "vec_start" in call_order
116:         # vec should start before graph finishes (parallel execution)
117:         assert call_order.index("vec_start") < call_order.index("graph_end")
118:

120: # =========================================================================
121: # B6: Release semaphore during retry sleep
122: # =========================================================================
123: 
124: 
125: class TestB6SemaphoreReleaseDuringRetry:
126:     """B6: Semaphore should be released before retry sleep."""
127: 
128:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
129:     async def test_semaphore_released_during_retry_sleep(self) -> None:
130:         """Semaphore slot is freed while sleeping between retries."""
131:         extractor = LLMEntityExtractor(model="test-model", max_retries=2, max_concurrent=1)
132: 
133:         mock_response = MagicMock()
134:         mock_response.choices = [MagicMock()]
135:         mock_response.choices[0].message.content = json.dumps(
136:             {"entities": [{"name": "Test", "entity_type": "CONCEPT"}], "relationships": []}
137:         )
138:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, completion_tokens=5, total_tokens=15)
139: 
140:         # First call fails, second succeeds
141:         call_count = 0
142: 
143:         async def mock_completion(**kwargs):
144:             nonlocal call_count
145:             call_count += 1
146:             if call_count == 1:
147:                 raise Exception("transient")
148:             return mock_response
149: 
150:         semaphore_was_free = False
151: 
152:         original_sleep = asyncio.sleep
153: 
154:         async def check_semaphore_during_sleep(duration):
155:             nonlocal semaphore_was_free
156:             # During sleep, the semaphore should be released
157:             # Try to acquire it to verify
158:             acquired = extractor._semaphore._value > 0
159:             if acquired:
160:                 semaphore_was_free = True
161:             await original_sleep(0)  # Don't actually sleep
162: 
163:         with (
164:             patch("litellm.acompletion", side_effect=mock_completion),
165:             patch("asyncio.sleep", side_effect=check_semaphore_during_sleep),
166:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
167:         ):
168:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
169:             result = await extractor.extract("test text")
170: 
171:         assert semaphore_was_free, "Semaphore should be free during retry sleep"
172:         assert len(result.entities) == 1
173: 
174:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
175:     async def test_extract_multi_semaphore_released_during_retry(self) -> None:
176:         """Multi-batch extraction also releases semaphore during retry."""
177:         extractor = LLMEntityExtractor(model="test-model", max_retries=2, max_concurrent=1)
178: 
179:         section_data = {"sections": [{"entities": [{"name": "A", "entity_type": "PERSON"}], "relationships": []}]}
180:         mock_response = MagicMock()
181:         mock_response.choices = [MagicMock()]
182:         mock_response.choices[0].message.content = json.dumps(section_data)
183:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, completion_tokens=5, total_tokens=15)
184: 
185:         call_count = 0
186: 
187:         async def mock_completion(**kwargs):
188:             nonlocal call_count
189:             call_count += 1
190:             if call_count == 1:
191:                 raise Exception("transient")
192:             return mock_response
193: 
194:         semaphore_was_free = False
195: 
196:         async def check_semaphore(duration):
197:             nonlocal semaphore_was_free
198:             if extractor._semaphore._value > 0:
199:                 semaphore_was_free = True
200: 
201:         with (
202:             patch("litellm.acompletion", side_effect=mock_completion),
203:             patch("asyncio.sleep", side_effect=check_semaphore),
204:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
205:         ):
206:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
207:             results = await extractor.extract_multi(["text1"], batch_size=5)
208: 
209:         assert semaphore_was_free, "Semaphore should be free during retry sleep"
210:         assert len(results) == 1
211:

213: # =========================================================================
214: # B11: Cache key computed once + B12: _parse_response accepts dict
215: # =========================================================================
216: 
217: 
218: class TestB11CacheKeyOptimization:
219:     """B11: Cache key should be computed once per text in embed_batch."""
220: 
221:     def test_cache_get_with_precomputed_key(self) -> None:
222:         """_cache_get accepts a pre-computed key."""
223:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
224:         key = embedder._cache_key("test")
225:         embedder._cache_put("test", [1.0, 2.0])
226:         result = embedder._cache_get("test", key=key)
227:         assert result == [1.0, 2.0]
228: 
229:     def test_cache_put_with_precomputed_key(self) -> None:
230:         """_cache_put accepts a pre-computed key."""
231:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
232:         key = embedder._cache_key("test")
233:         embedder._cache_put("test", [1.0, 2.0], key=key)
234:         result = embedder._cache_get("test")
235:         assert result == [1.0, 2.0]
236: 
237:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
238:     async def test_embed_batch_uses_precomputed_keys(self) -> None:
239:         """embed_batch should compute keys once (verified by cache working correctly)."""
240:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", max_retries=1)
241: 
242:         mock_response = MagicMock()
243:         mock_response.data = [{"embedding": [0.1, 0.2]}]
244:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, total_tokens=10)
245: 
246:         with (
247:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response),
248:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
249:         ):
250:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
251:             result = await embedder.embed_batch(["hello"])
252: 
253:         assert result == [[0.1, 0.2]]
254:         # Verify it was cached
255:         assert embedder._cache_get("hello") == [0.1, 0.2]
256: 
257: 
258: class TestB12ParseResponseAcceptsDict:
259:     """B12: _parse_response should accept pre-parsed dicts (no JSON round-trip)."""
260: 
261:     def test_parse_response_with_dict(self) -> None:
262:         """_parse_response accepts a dict directly."""
263:         extractor = LLMEntityExtractor(model="test-model")
264:         data = {
265:             "entities": [{"name": "Alice", "entity_type": "PERSON", "description": "A person"}],
266:             "relationships": [],
267:         }
268:         result = extractor._parse_response(data)
269:         assert len(result.entities) == 1
270:         assert result.entities[0].name == "Alice"
271: 
272:     def test_parse_response_with_string(self) -> None:
273:         """_parse_response still works with JSON strings."""
274:         extractor = LLMEntityExtractor(model="test-model")
275:         data = {
276:             "entities": [{"name": "Bob", "entity_type": "ORGANIZATION"}],
277:             "relationships": [],
278:         }
279:         result = extractor._parse_response(json.dumps(data))
280:         assert len(result.entities) == 1
281:         assert result.entities[0].name == "Bob"
282: 
283:     def test_parse_response_dict_with_events(self) -> None:
284:         """Dict input with events parses correctly."""
285:         extractor = LLMEntityExtractor(model="test-model")
286:         data = {
287:             "entities": [],
288:             "relationships": [],
289:             "events": [{"description": "Meeting", "event_type": "EVENT"}],
290:         }
291:         result = extractor._parse_response(data)
292:         assert len(result.events) == 1
293: 
294:     def test_parse_response_dict_with_temporal(self) -> None:
295:         """Dict input with temporal info parses correctly."""
296:         extractor = LLMEntityExtractor(model="test-model")
297:         data = {
298:             "entities": [
299:                 {
300:                     "name": "Meeting",
301:                     "entity_type": "EVENT",
302:                     "temporal": {"mentioned_at": "2024-01-01"},
303:                 }
304:             ],
305:             "relationships": [],
306:         }
307:         result = extractor._parse_response(data)
308:         assert result.entities[0].temporal is not None

311: # =========================================================================
312: # B10: Embedding input deduplication
313: # =========================================================================
314: 
315: 
316: class TestB10EmbeddingDeduplication:
317:     """B10: Duplicate texts in embed_batch should only be embedded once."""
318: 
319:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
320:     async def test_duplicate_texts_single_api_call(self) -> None:
321:         """Same text appearing multiple times only gets embedded once."""
322:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", batch_size=100, max_retries=1)
323: 
324:         mock_response = MagicMock()
325:         # Only ONE embedding returned because dedup reduces to 1 unique text
326:         mock_response.data = [{"embedding": [0.1, 0.2]}]
327:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=5, total_tokens=5)
328: 
329:         with (
330:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response) as mock_api,
331:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
332:         ):
333:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
334:             result = await embedder.embed_batch(["same text", "same text", "same text"])
335: 
336:         # All three should get the same embedding
337:         assert len(result) == 3
338:         assert result[0] == [0.1, 0.2]
339:         assert result[1] == [0.1, 0.2]
340:         assert result[2] == [0.1, 0.2]
341: 
342:         # API should have been called with only 1 unique text
343:         call_args = mock_api.call_args
344:         assert len(call_args.kwargs["input"]) == 1
345: 
346:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
347:     async def test_mixed_unique_and_duplicate(self) -> None:
348:         """Mix of unique and duplicate texts deduplicates correctly."""
349:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", batch_size=100, max_retries=1)
350: 
351:         mock_response = MagicMock()
352:         # 2 unique texts → 2 embeddings
353:         mock_response.data = [
354:             {"embedding": [0.1, 0.2]},
355:             {"embedding": [0.3, 0.4]},
356:         ]
357:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, total_tokens=10)
358: 
359:         with (
360:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response) as mock_api,
361:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
362:         ):
363:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
364:             result = await embedder.embed_batch(["alpha", "beta", "alpha"])
365: 
366:         assert len(result) == 3
367:         assert result[0] == [0.1, 0.2]  # alpha
368:         assert result[1] == [0.3, 0.4]  # beta
369:         assert result[2] == [0.1, 0.2]  # alpha (dedup)
370: 
371:         # Only 2 unique texts sent to API
372:         call_args = mock_api.call_args
373:         assert len(call_args.kwargs["input"]) == 2
374: 
375:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
376:     async def test_dedup_with_cache(self) -> None:
377:         """Deduplication works alongside cache."""
378:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", batch_size=100, max_retries=1)
379:         embedder._cache_put("cached", [0.5, 0.6])
380: 
381:         mock_response = MagicMock()
382:         # 1 unique uncached text
383:         mock_response.data = [{"embedding": [0.7, 0.8]}]
384:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=5, total_tokens=5)
385: 
386:         with (
387:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response) as mock_api,
388:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
389:         ):
390:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
391:             result = await embedder.embed_batch(["cached", "new_text", "new_text"])
392: 
393:         assert result[0] == [0.5, 0.6]  # from cache
394:         assert result[1] == [0.7, 0.8]  # from API
395:         assert result[2] == [0.7, 0.8]  # dedup'd from API
396: 
397:         # Only 1 text sent to API (new_text, deduplicated)
398:         call_args = mock_api.call_args
399:         assert len(call_args.kwargs["input"]) == 1
400: 
401:

403: # B2: Batch entity upsert (replaces N+1)

406: 
407: class TestB2BatchEntityUpsert:
408:     """B2: upsert_entities_batch should handle both graph and vector in parallel."""
409: 
410:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
411:     async def test_upsert_batch_parallel(self) -> None:
412:         """Batch upsert uses asyncio.gather for graph+vector."""
413:         ns_id = uuid4()
414:         entity = MagicMock(spec=Entity, namespace_id=ns_id, id=uuid4())
415:         entities = [entity]
416: 
417:         graph = MagicMock()
418:         graph.upsert_entities_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=[(entity, True)])
419:         vec = MagicMock()
420:         vec.upsert_entities_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=[(entity, True)])
421: 
422:         coord = StorageCoordinator(graph=graph, vector=vec)
423:         with patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem:
424:             mock_telem.return_value.record_storage_op = MagicMock()
425:             results = await coord.upsert_entities_batch(ns_id, entities)
426: 
427:         assert len(results) == 1
428:         graph.upsert_entities_batch.assert_awaited_once()
429:         vec.upsert_entities_batch.assert_awaited_once()
430: 
431:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
432:     async def test_upsert_batch_graph_only(self) -> None:
433:         """Batch upsert works with graph backend only."""
434:         ns_id = uuid4()
435:         entity = MagicMock(spec=Entity)
436:         entities = [entity]
437: 
438:         graph = MagicMock()
439:         graph.upsert_entities_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=[(entity, True)])
440: 
441:         coord = StorageCoordinator(graph=graph)
442:         with patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem:
443:             mock_telem.return_value.record_storage_op = MagicMock()
444:             results = await coord.upsert_entities_batch(ns_id, entities)
445: 
446:         assert len(results) == 1
447:

449: # =========================================================================
450: # B8: Batch relationship storage in expansion
451: # =========================================================================
452: 
453: 
454: class TestB8BatchRelationshipsInExpansion:
455:     """B8: Expansion should use create_relationships_batch instead of individual calls."""
456: 
457:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
458:     async def test_store_expansion_results_batches_relationships(self) -> None:
459:         """store_expansion_results calls create_relationships_batch."""
460:         # Import the underlying function, bypassing Prefect @task
461:         from khora.pipelines.flows.expansion import store_expansion_results
462: 
463:         storage = MagicMock()
464:         storage.update_entity = AsyncMock()
465:         storage.create_relationships_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=3)
466: 
467:         rels = [MagicMock(), MagicMock(), MagicMock()]
468: 
469:         # Call the raw function, not the Prefect task wrapper
470:         result_obj = MagicMock()
471:         result_obj.merged_entity_count = 0
472:         result_obj.entity_mapping = {}
473:         result_obj.inferred_relationships = rels
474: 
475:         stats = await store_expansion_results(result_obj, storage)
476: 
477:         storage.create_relationships_batch.assert_awaited_once_with(rels)
478:         assert stats["stored_relationships"] == 3
479: 
480:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
481:     async def test_store_expansion_no_relationships(self) -> None:
482:         """No relationships means no batch call."""
483:         from khora.pipelines.flows.expansion import store_expansion_results
484: 
485:         storage = MagicMock()
486:         storage.update_entity = AsyncMock()
487:         storage.create_relationships_batch = AsyncMock()
488: 
489:         result_obj = MagicMock()
490:         result_obj.merged_entity_count = 0
491:         result_obj.entity_mapping = {}
492:         result_obj.inferred_relationships = []
493: 
494:         await store_expansion_results(result_obj, storage)
495:         storage.create_relationships_batch.assert_not_awaited()
496:
````
